title: Old town the place to be in Quilto
text: Old Town in Quilto is amazing to walk around and enjoy, very much like some of the European on town with the narrow street with cobblestones.Even more impressive when you consider the slopes of streets being in Andes very hilly.All areas in old town are within walking distance so it’s just great to walk around and get lost in the streets, nice variety of shops, restaurants, cafes to enjoy.

title: Very easily explorable on foot.
text: We wondered through the old city of Quito on foot. - exploring, taking it in and enjoying. We, of course, visited the old square, several churches, boutiques and stores. We enjoyed delicious coffee in cafes- like bodega di Cantuna. Several monuments drew our attention.It is a delightful colonial old city easily walkable and navigable.

title: Travel Responsibly
text: Quito: where the sky touches the earth.Quito: where the sky touches the earth.The best of Ecuador to travel and get to know all the architecture and how modern and ancient the city is

title: A very interesting old town
text: We spent three days walking in Quito, and the city really seduced us. The historic centre and the old town are a real colonial jewel, with cobblestone streets, lively squares and colourful facades. Quito, founded in 1534 on the remains of ancient Inca cities, is full of history and architecture: baroque churches such as the Compañía or San Francisco are incredible, often richly decorated and very photogenic. Local markets bring a lively and authentic touch, with crafts, fruit, and local specialties. Every corner offers a discovery, and walking allows you to feel the unique atmosphere of this city that combines heritage, culture and daily life. A real favorite for history and architecture lovers!

title: Historic Center
text: We really enjoyed walking around the Quito main square and other parts of the historic center.  There were some nice spots for photos and it was not particularly crowded.  There were a few vendors including those with tables set up to sell hand crafts as well as some stands selling various candies.  I do recommend going with a guide so that you can gain an understanding.  Overall, a very good experience.

title: Definetely a must when in Quito!
text: Quito Old Town is beautiful, there are so many different churches you can visit and most of them offer you guided tours aswell. I reccomend taking cash as most places do not accept card.

title: Fascinating Old Town, Be Alert After Sundown
text: I really liked Quito's old town. During the day, the streets felt alive with people and daily activities. Stores and restaurants were busy. The old town was interesting and atmospheric.After sunset, the activity ebbed; though, eateries were still open. I felt safe on the main streets; but, some of the dimly lit alleyways looked empty and ominous.

title: Very well preserved.
text: The historic center of Quito is really well preserved, with large squares and many churches, first of all the Basilica and the church of the Society of Jesus. But there are also many hidden gems such as the Bolivar theater and calle la Ronda to give an example.A note of merit to the craft beer that you can find in the convent of San Francisco and the palace of the archbishopric that houses a famous restaurant and a curious history.A note of discord is only for the lack of security with which one enters the high parts of some churches, really dangerous.

title: Please be aware of thieves.
text: My wife and I had one day to spend in Quito on our way to the Galapagos. We stayed on the edge of the Old Town and on Sunday morning walked a seemingly safe and obvious route from Plaza de San Francisco to Independence Square and on up to the Basilica of the National Vow. Sunny day, lots of people about but as we walked up Venezuela to the Basilica, it seemed a bird poohed on my wife from above. A seemingly helpful man carrying a water bottle stared to pour water over some of the ‘poop’. An older man seemingly just walking up the road too pointed upwards to indicate this must have been a bird. It wasn’t. They had thrown a mucky bird-poop like mixture onto my wife and while we were distracted stole the rucksack I had stupidly put on the ground against a wall - they actually swapped an empty black  rucksack for ours which meant it took us a little longer to realise we’d been robbed. We are experienced travellers and thought we were being very careful - beware of the speed and ingenuity of Quito thieves.

title: Private Guide of Quito
text: Spent a full day in Quito before we head to the Galapagos tomorrow for our Lindblad Expeditions National Geographic Expeditions cruise. Through the excellent advice and recommendations from the group we hired Ricardo Villalobos as our guide for the day. He picked us up at the Swissotel (also highly recommend) at 8:45 and for almost 9 hours took us to all the top sites on our list: the middle of the earth/equator, the Church of the Society of Jesus (golden church), Independence Square, lunch at La Vid ( fantastic!), Virgin of the Panecillo on the hill with spectacular views, chocolate shopping, La Ronda ( would only go there with a guide), and shopping at the indoor markets. Ricardo is extremely professional, knowledgeable, accommodating, seriously knows his way around the city, and went above and beyond to cover everything we wanted to see. I felt COMPLETELY safe with him and would not tour the city on my own. Traffic is crazy and neighborhoods can change quickly. His car is super clean and comfortable. And he’s extremely reasonable. He has excellent reviews on Trip Advisor which are well earned. I highly highly recommend him! Lastly, every single Ecuadorian we met was friendly, gracious and welcoming. They are lovely people!”

title: Not accessible!!!
text: We went to old town on Saturday January 5th and most of the main tourist streets were completely barricaded off. About 3 blocks in every direction Plaza Grande was blocked off by steel barricades and police but there was no signage on why it wasn’t accessible.  There was also a language barrier so we couldn’t figure out why no one could go in the main part of the historic district. Petty big bummer since we only has one day there.

title: Cool spot but be cautious
text: A beautiful area to walk around with many amazing cathedrals and historic buildings. We toured a few churches. There were also a few people dressed up as characters that kids/fans might enjoy. Most spots seemed to be cash only but prices are low.Please use basic safety precautions. If you're taking pictures with your phone, keep a firm grip or have someone 'spot' you. You'll need small bills/dollar coins to enter most of the attractions. Make sure to store cash safely on your person. Avoid being out after dark. Even the locals do this so best to follow their lead.

title: Walk in the historic center
text: The historic center of Quito is simply charming! There are many ancient churches, which are worth visiting to see the details of the buildings. It is also worth walking to La Ronda, a gastronomic place where you can meet various restaurants and bars. The church museum is also worth visiting and at the end of the tour enjoy the craft beer.

title: Be vigilent of your surroundings
text: Our guide walked us down the Street of Seven Crosses and gave us a brief history of each of the seven churches along the street.  We then walked through Independence Square and then onto Plaza de San Francisco, and final a visit to Yumbos chocolate factory. The colonial architecture was amazing and everywhere was busy with locals and tourists, with many artists set up on the side of the streets selling paintings, knitted garments and much more. Plenty of police around on every corner so we felt relatively safe.

title: So crazy busy, hard to enjoy
text: While beautiful and historic, the beauty is ruined with graffiti and pan handlers. Many of the old buildings are covered in graffiti, the streets are extremely crowded, and everyone local (hotel staff and  taxi drivers) warned us to be very careful, and not to even display our cell phones because the theft is so bad. Even when in 2 different taxi the drivers told us to put our phone away while in the taxi. Very uncomfortable feeling.The constant worry ruined the beauty for me.  We spent about an hour and got out.  The traffic to get to old town is also crazy. Took nearly 20 minutes to get 6 blocks.

title: Not incredible.
text: The historic center of Quito, is the same as any historic center. Really nothing special. Watch out for the pickpoket.

title: Great Experience
text: Interesting history of the quito old town.We enjoyed walking the old town.Many nice places to eat and shop.worth visiting.

title: Safe and interesting
text: Really worth seeing, take a guide and listen about interesting history of Quito and also about nowadays situation, troubles, politics. You can manage almost all the major places in half day / day. Enjoy a cup of cocoa on your way

title: Exploring Quito through sights and tastes
text: Walking through Quito's Old Town is indeed a delightful experience. The cobblestone streets add a historic charm, transporting you back in time. The well-preserved buildings, with their colonial architecture and intricate details, showcase the city's rich history and cultural heritage. Around each corner, you’ll find quaint places to explore and enjoy a taste of their world-famous chocolate paired with delicious pastries. Quito will not only immerse you in visual sights but also in tasteful charm.

title: Voyage in South America
text: Beautiful, stylish city, colonial buildings, valuables left in the hotel, so walked freely, in the historical center lot of police. All very beautiful, lots of cafes

title: Beautiful walk for the family
text: For the weekend to escape a moment to tour the historic center is beautiful, the ease of the metro made us arrive in 7 minutes, the weather accompanied us and we could enjoy the churches and the round! Do not hesitate to visit if you have no plans for a weekend!!

title: One of the most beautiful in Latin America
text: In the great and chaotic capital there is this historic center that really left us speechless, both because the buildings, squares and various churches are preserved very well, but also for the atmosphere with colonial flavors and for the beautiful natural setting that surrounds it: in my opinion one of the most beautiful historic centers in a capital that I have seen in South and Central America! Traffic is a bit 'felt in the main arteries and at 2,850 m the smog is even heavier (never as much as in Cuenca!), but nevertheless inside are located purely pedestrian areas or limited traffic and so the problem I have not felt too much. The same goes for security: during the day it is heavily manned by police and police auxiliaries, so much so that I have never felt uncomfortable walking around (of course, as everywhere in the big cities of the world you always have to pay attention). Then I must say that Ecuadorians are fantastic people, kind and always available!

title: Very interesting visit
text: We spent 5 days mainly walking around the Old Town. The architecture is very interesting and historic. The chuiches magnificent. Great eating places with lots of interesting shops and chocolate tasting etc.Really worth spending time here

title: World Heritage Site
text: A world heritage site, the Historic Center of Quito preserves the colonial characteristics of the "master plan" of 1700: elongated and low houses, with patios, which extend onto the streets which, although on uneven terrain, reproduce whenever possible, a chessboard. And then the squares, with the central Plaza Major, and the churches and convents often examples of the Baroque school of Quito (Escuela Quitena), which brings together indigenous and European artistic traditions and which is renowned for having provided America's greatest contribution Spanish to universal art.I must admit that such beauty, however, did not move me deeply as it happened in other historic centers.

title: Beautiful historic center very well preserved and rich in history and art and excellent restaurants
text: Dedicate some time to this visit, better with an expert guide, so as not to miss the details of this beautiful historic center. Founded on Inca ruins, among colonial buildings and well-maintained squares, we were amazed at how clean and well-kept it was, the small balconies full of pots of geraniums were beautiful, but be careful of pickpockets and use caution in the evening. The interior of the churches is sumptuous, such as the Jesuit church of the Society of Jesus of Quito (Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesús) decorated with gold leaf ornaments, magnificent gilded ceilings, beautiful paintings and the gold-plated main altar. It took 160 years to complete Ecuador's most decorated church. The Church of San Francesco with its beautiful square and the convent are also worth visiting, as is the cathedral which overlooks the beautiful Plaza de la Independencia or the Gothic basilica, without forgetting the museums and excellent restaurants.

title: Must see.
text: Must visit old town. Is crowded compared to all other areas of Quito but very walkable. Plenty of police but still be aware of people around you.

title: Great on Sundays!
text: On a Sunday, the old town is closed to cars and you can have your fill of all the stalls in the streets and of course eat great food. Nice atmosphere and lots of music make it an experience.

title: It is an experience that feedbacks our knowledge.
text: It is very beautiful, you are filled with knowledge, an experience that you will like, recommended and if you want to take a tour with a professional tourist guide. Contact me 593 991350512.

title: Walking through the center during the day is a "must" if you visit Quito
text: The center is beautiful with many historic buildings and charming hidden patios. During the day the streets are bustling with people and the center is a giant market where you can find everything. After 6pm the center becomes empty, it is not dangerous but without the hustle and bustle it loses much of its charm.

title: Lots to look at...
text: It's just nice to walk around here... You just have to be careful, but there's no reason to feel unsafe.There is a lot to look at if you just walk around quietly. Lots of beautiful buildings, cafes and restaurants. Some of the restaurants have a roof terrace, with an absolutely fantastic view of Quito.Remember good shoes, a hat and sunscreen!We had our own guide, from the Andean travel company. Which is highly recommended.

title: Lovely Square
text: Sitting in the square by the San Francisco Church was a lovely way to spend an afternoon. Lots of street food on offer , the devil preaching ! Wedding parties and children having a great time. People watching with a coffee and there was a fantastic atmosphere

title: Fascinating City
text: We really enjoyed our stay in Quito.  The Old City is fascinating and there are so many good restaurants.  We had a private guide and recommend doing a guided tour. The churches are spectacular and and not to be missed.  Spend time ambling the streets to get a good feel for the City.

title: Quito, Carita de Dios.
text: Quito Old Town is the best Spanish Old Town in the Americas!It has overwhelming architecture, its churches are amazing.It has a natural outlook (El Panecillo hill) from where you can observe the city and its majestic Andes!Every time I go to Quito I continue to fall in love with this unique city surrounded by green beautiful mountains and snow capped peaks!

title: Such a special place
text: I am not surprised that Quito Old Town is in the list of the Unesco Heritage Sites. The colonial Spanish feel is all over the place with narrow streets and vast squares and the miriads of churches and convents. The walks up and down the three Main Streets (Quayaqil, Venezuela and Garcia Moreno) are full of little shops with interesting local artefacts and little restaurants and in the afternoon full of buskers and little stands of local craft and the music is coming from all over the places!Quito do not have the grandeur of other Colonial Spanish cities (like Havana or Old Panama) but I love the cosyness of the low raise buildings and the pastel colour of the houses.Certainly a wonderful discovery.

title: Well worth a walk around
text: Well worth a walk around, lovely old buildings, hole in the wall style shops, fabulous architecture. Do this walk through on te way to the square after the main church area. Particularly nice on a Sunday!

title: Cultural meld
text: Old Quito combines a lot of the style of both Baroque Spain and South America.  There are several plazas and wide pedestrian roads and every corner has an ornate church or palace on it.  Quito and Ecuador are not wealthy, but downtown Quito was vibrant and people were friendly, and although it was lively, it wasn't too crowded.  I got some sense that Quito could be dangerous, or at least that theft was a problem.  But I did not feel any sense of menace while I was in Old Quito (or other downtown areas).  There's a heavy police presence and the crowd seemed to be mostly tourists, school groups and downtown workers.

title: Music, dances, beautiful outfits in the Historic Center!!
text: Living the holidays and the expressive Viva Quito:)!!Touring this beautiful center in this important and special time!!Traditional music, dances, beautiful colorful costumes!!!Very happy atmosphere.

title: Worth a visit, it is the Old Town After all
text: You're in Quito and as with many cities the Old Town is a nice place to take in some history and enjoy the architecture and Quito is no different.  I was there during the daytime and with a guide who told me to flip my backpack to the front and carry it on my chest as well as moving and securing anything (wallet, etc.) in my pockets to my backpack where it was tightly under my control.  She is a local and a native so followed the advice and had no problems.  I felt safe in general but do appreciate that she was looking out for me.

title: Nice place
text: Our experience in old quito was nice even though was a bit hot.The church was the best part of the visit and worthwhile to see.

title: Beatiful and Historic with caveats
text: Quito's Centro Historico is considered one of the best preserved and largest colonial centers in the Americas, filled with buildings that are three hundred plus years old. Ornate churches seem to dot every corner.  So many of the buildings are simply works of art. Plazas located in the center are lively filled with locals, vendors, and tourists, at least during the day. There are a number of museums one can visit, along with numerous restaurants and stores.One caveat is generally speaking at night the old town is largely deserted and feels unsafe/seedy (other than the Plaza Independencia/presidential place area and La Ronda on weekends).  Another is even during day one must be somewhat careful, we were told by many people to mind our wallets, don't keep cellphones out and keep our wits in general. There is a sizeable homeless presence with some coming relatively close begging for money, however there is a large police presence during the day, overall it felt safe. In the end nothing happened to us and we enjoyed our visit.The old town is among the highlights of Quito, and should be visited, travel in groups if you can, don't wander alone at night, just be mindful of your surroundings and you should be fine.

title: A walk into history
text: The old city is amazingly preserved. Loved walking the streets, and seeing the churches, cathedrals, and courtyards. A must visit on your trip to Ecuador.

title: Full of character and safe for this solo traveller
text: First off, I never had any problems with pickpockets nor did I feel unsafe at anytime.  Like any other city, just be sensible ie don't wander alone at night in unlit areas, don't have an expensive camera just in your hand with no wrist or neck strap, in general don't make it easy for any potential thief.  I also found that many locals did know some English and were happy to help with directions for instance.It is a charming area to have a walk around, but be warned it is hilly and may be challenging if not yet accustomed to the high altitude as well.Plenty of stunning architecture to admire, especially inside the churches, also park areas with seats for resting up, lots of restaurants and cafes and souvenir shops.  Also many pet shops which I found was a theme throughout my trip of Ecuador, not sure why?!!

title: How to see Old City
text: We visited Quito while waiting to see the Galapagos islands.  The history of the old city was worth the visit.  We took one tour to see the country side and walked the city on our own.  I would highly recommend arranging a local tour so that you see more of the old city.  The tours outside the city involve an hour plus drive each way.  It was interesting but not a polished experience.  I would try the tours by locals if going again. The old city is just that—very old.  Streets are often narrow and often still paved with hand carved stones.  Be prepared to walk and to walk up hill.  It was a great stop over on our trip to the islands.We stayed at La Casana.  Hoetel and staff were great.

title: Don't Hesitate
text: Don't hesitate to visit this beautiful part of the city. In the daytime you are very safe, with normal considerations about keeping your valuables nearby. At night be sure to travel carefully with 2 or more together and stay away from dark alleys. It is a fun and interesting part o the city.

title: Take a few good masks!
text: Quito is huge noisy, busy, horrible pollution! Couldn’t wait to leave. Couple hours in old city was about churches, yet I enjoyed 5 minutes of a  couple men playing classical guitar on the street. For a country/city that depends on tourism I’m surprised how very few speak English. Means you hire a guide and their English isn’t as understandable as the price indicates, or their quality of information. Sorry, I was disappointed after months of planning.

title: Crazy traffic
text: Very very busy. My uber driver and I sat in traffic for at least an hour. Be mindful of when you go because traffic is insane. Other than having a delicious gelato, I didnt really enjoy my time here.

title: BE AWARE QUITO PICKPOCKETS
text: BE AWARE. Quito has gangs of pickpockets operating. We were targeted 4 times in a week. The main trick is to covertly spray you from behind with a mud solution that looks a bit like bird droppings. They then point it out and pull a handful of serviettes from their pocket and "help" you clean up. Whilst doing this they pick your pocket.Often others will join in to "help" BE AWARE do not let anyone help you. They were all well dressed middle aged and spoke good english.

title: Gorgeous!
text: This was my favorite area of Quito.  The buildings were amazing, the cathedrals stunning, and the overall look of the area was just gorgeous!  People are friendly and the streets lively, bustling during the days filled with vendors, locals and tourists.

title: Very Hilly
text: A very useful thing to know about Quito is that it is very hilly. When you plan a trip to this superb Andean capitol, knowing it is hilly will be key to your preparation. If it isn't, it should be

title: Old World Charm
text: Unesco world heritage site, for many good reasons. The architecture from colonial times is fascinating, especially the churches and monasteries. Streets have some moderate inclines, so with altitude, take your time strolling. Police/security presence is noticeable and gives a good sense of personal safety. Numerous street hawkers selling all types of goods, but they don't pester the tourists.

title: Nice busy area in the day
text: A very pleasant area to stroll around, well daytime at least. Many churches and old buildings to admire, Grand Plaza etc and particularly so one afternoon with a ceremony, dress uniforms on horseback and band in front of the Palacio de Carondelet. Less attractive at night as it is very quiet although we had no issues strolling from our local hotel to restaurants.

title: Well worth the walk
text: We followed the Lonely Planet walking tour. Starting at Grande Plaza we meandered backwards and forwards through the streets. Stopping off at churches, museums and coffee places. If you are in Quito don’t miss the opportunity to visit this area.

title: Great few day layover to Galapagos
text: We stayed for a total of 3 nights on either side of our amazing trip to Galapagos Islands and we were veryglad we did.  The old town area is very large with so many beautiful original Spanish Colonial buildingschurches and plazas with amazing history.   There are so many great restaurants and bars too with amazingviews.   The altitude may make you short of breath on walking up hill at first but the views at this altitude - 9000ft making it the highest capital in the world) are splendid.

title: A Must Visit Destination In Quito
text: You can't not visit the Historic District of Quito when visiting the City.The San Francisco Square, Historic Buildings, Museums, Historical Churches, Shopping, Markets, Restaurants and more, can all be found in the bustling and very popular part of Quito City.

title: Old town Quito
text: We walked around this quaint area without a guide because we only had a few hours. We discovered some beautiful monuments. It was starting to rain and we found a restaurant called Cafe Del Fraile in a building near the large square near the San Francisco monastery cathedral. We were able to get a taxi easily to and from the hotel. We later found out there was a hop on hop off bus that we could’ve gone on but we only had one day in the city.

title: Magical visit 2 the past
text: Immerse yourself in a pumping cityBreathtaking Architecture Kind interested polite peopleBeautiful intricate crafts

title: Quito, Ecuador
text: It was a beautiful city with old hotels and old churches. We stayed at the Friends Hotel, a beautiful, old, quaint hotel with the best staff and fantastic breakfasts! We had cake and coffee at the Artifice Cafeteria right down the street for another great experience.The down side was the thieves. Two of our group were robbed in the first two days we were there. Pickpockets and thieves are around every corner. Keep your purses closed and in your hand and wear your back packs on your front.

title: Huge Old Town
text: The old town of Quito is quite large and  is not like man other old towns.  While many of the old building have been perserved, there is a lot of business going on.  If in Quito then it is definitely a must do to spend time in the old town.  There are several beautiful churches and plazas to visit .  All can be seen in 4 hours.  There are many museums and here i would suggest to choose the ones which are of interest.  Real art / artisen goods are limited in the old town - most of the shops are basic goods for everyday use.  If looking for high quality goods then go to Casa Latina in the new town.

title: A wonderful experience
text: Quito is a big and beautiful city. To walk across its streets is a wonderful experience, you can find a lot of places where to eat traditional food or have some fun learning about the culture and historical buildings, but you will need some time if you want to discover all the wonders about this beautiful city. So I highly recommend to take a look at this city in your next holidays.

title: All Kinds of Shops
text: Old town has everything and all types of shops.  My favorites were the food shops selling rice, beans, spices, and the products needed for cooking every day.

title: Quito with Private Tour Operator.
text: Great Experience  of Old town Quito with Anywhere Ecuador. Our Guide Ricardo took us on the walking tour of all the landmarks explaining the history of Ecuador Independence.

title: Visit in Quito.
text: Nice place to walk and visit interested places.  Lot of nice small coffee houses and restaurants. Lot of historical old buildings to be explored.

title: Beautiful old town, but always felt on my guard
text: Our wonderful old hotel, up on a hill, was very close by the Old Town. So we were in the historic center many times on different occasions during our stay. I was aware of safety issues, so I tried to take the necessary precautions. I never did get 'victimized' but there was at least one occasion where I could see a 'set up' taking place involving two other people. This occurred close to the entrance of Calle La Ronda. The moment I sensed it, we simply walked away briskly towards more populated areas. And we got acquainted with some other tourists that had some unfortunate experiences. One was an attempted robbery at La Ronda, which started as a 'request for payment' just to walk on the street of La Ronda. Another couple we ran into were targeted for a scam in La Carolina park. In this situation, they spray you from behind, leading you to believe a bird pooped on your, then try to get 'touchy-feely' to help clean you up, all the while pick-pocketing. Based on some reviews, it sounds like the situation is worse in post-pandemic times. Also note that operating hours for business and tourist attractions are unreliable. Don't count on websites, travel books, or locals to give you accurate information. We could not even get accurate info from the street police, or the tourist info center. For example, the 'changing of the guard' advertised in travel books is no longer taking place since the pandemic. And yet many locals told us that it was. We only knew for sure by showing up at the designated time, and nothing was happening. Note that this was my experience in the summer of 2022.

title: Too bad that it gets very desolate after 6 pm
text: The churches are beautiful.  Had coffee and bunuelos at Hotel Patio Andaluz-- great coffee as well as service.

title: Beautiful but dangerous
text: Main plaza is safe as it's packing with cops, the rest of the Centro historico is dangerous full of prostitute's, muggers and undesirables hanging around , which is a shame as the place has some real nice old buildings to visit, Ecuador was once one if the safer countries in the region but sadly not anymore as the inequality gap has has stretched considerably.

title: Very fun time.
text: Very nice time.  The architecture was very interesting. The square was vibrant.  The restaurants were great.  They say that you haven't seen Quito until you've been to the Old Town,  and this seems to be correct.

title: The city took me back in time
text: The city takes you back in time because it has so much history, it is also one of a kind UNESCO World Heritage Site. One can explore the city on foot, see new galleries, and smell amazing freshly baked goods on each corner. There are some really interesting museums, shops and churches. The most people-friendly place and safe to explore the city.

title: Great experience
text: This place has a rich history and you can feel it while taking a tour around the historic district. I really enjoyed exploring it!

title: Very fun and safe place to walk and explore
text: There are pickpockets, but most people are friendly and open for you to browse and explore,  Most people will only speak Spanish though.

title: Nice for exploring around
text: There is a lot of history here so it would be worth it to take a tour around the historic district. The whole historic district was named a UNESCO World Heritage Site so it's definitely worth going to.

title: Old Town
text: Wasn't super impressed, parts were crazy packed and people going all directions. We've been to Chinatown in Bangkok on a weekend. Views from on top of the churches were amazing. We're going to try a walking guided tour, was a bit hard solo. Churches were well worth the effort but hindsight I would Uber to each spot maybe.

title: Well preserved Spanish colonial center
text: Quito's compact colonial center is one of the best preserved in Latin America.  Worth a stroll and definitely worth entering some of the amazing churches and other colonial buildings.  Nothing 5 star here but a really nice escape from the rest of the city.

title: The old town is a must when visit Quito
text: The old town is very historic and filled with buildings from 300 to 500 years old.  You can walk the town.   Traffic is bad in this city,

title: Sprawling but worth it
text: Quito’s 600 year old old town is sprawling. Mosh mash of beautifully restored churches, palace, Monastery, as well as electronics shops, souvenirs, etc. Follow orange flowers painted in asphalt to reach center of town. Affordable lunches all over, even with the best views.

title: Interesting
text: A tremendous amount of history within the blocks of the square - much to see and do.  Felt safe due to the number of police in the area.  (We were there during numerous rallies/protests)

title: Beauty and history at the doorstep of the Andes.
text: Somewhat free from the restrictions imposed by the COVID pandemic, and initially scheduled for April of 2020, my wife and I finally embarked on our South American trip to Quito and the Galapagos Islands, in the beautiful country of Ecuador.Quito is a city worth seeing but we highly recommend using a guide. It will cost you about 100$ per person (if you are a couple), per day, but it will save time and frustration trying to make your way from one point of interest to another. We were immensely satisfied with the services of our guide from “Tours by Local”. Do remember that Quito is a high-altitude city of close to 2850 meters (9350ft.), so take it slow and pace yourselves. For some, higher elevations may mean greater difficulty in breathing, sleeping, headaches, or a combination of all. From old Spanish colonial and religious sites, to overseeing the city from the mountain top, tasting your way through culinary delights, and above all people that are friendly and kind, Quito has much to offer for the quasi “off-beaten-path” traveler. As Quito is no different from any other city of importance throughout the world, vigilance is recommended.  Some quarters should not be walked alone (as a couple or individually) after dark. Otherwise, have an experience of a life time.

title: Worth an Afternoon's Exploration
text: This UNESCO World Heritage Site showcases 16th century Spanish culture with many churches, the seat of government, narrow walkways, and people of different cultures in a very walkable area.

title: COLONIAL ARCHITECTURE
text: A most interesting walking tour amidst Spanish colonial architecture. I've seen it in other countries. I never get tired of being in the midst of it. Well worth the time spent here.

title: Beautifully preserved old colonial town
text: I have been to a ton of different cities colonized by the Spanish that lead to a similar type of architecture more or less - Bogotá, Cartagena, Lima, Arequipa, Havana, San Juan, Granada, León, Ciudad de Panamá, Ciudad de México, Mérida, etc.. I can confidently say that Quito has some of the most extensive and well preserved networks of buildings. There's a reason it's a UNESCO World Heritage Site! I would recommend spending weekdays here as that's when it's most busy - on the weekends it gets kind of desolate and there aren't a lot of businesses open.

title: So many interesting places to see
text: A world heritage city with so many unique buildings and squares to visit. The busy traffics and polluted air was an issue but Quito is a great place to admire the colonial architecture and churches.

title: A highly recommended experience of Old Town
text: I really enjoyed the experience of Old Town, Quito.  The architecture, the cobble streets, the history, food, culture... all of it was very enjoyable.  We had a guided walking tour that lasted about 3+ hours.  Our guide was incredibly knowledgable and took us to some great places.  Most people are very friendly and always smiled at me when I smiled at them.  Other than a few tasteless men cat-calling (whom I ignored), I didn't experience any negative interactions.  I was carrying around my large, Canon SLR, which did make me a little uneasy, but I was with 5 other people (including my husband).  The guide said that the pandemic has made poverty and crime worse in the city and that pick-pocketing can happen if you leave your wallet or cell-phone exposed.  He said that as long as we were keeping our valuables secure, we should be okay.  There were a couple areas he did call out as being unsafe during the night and so we should avoid.  Right now (Jan/Feb 2022), for anyone considering a visit soon, they do require you to show your vaccination card to enter many places so one good recommendation our guide gave was to take a photo of your important documents, your ID, passport, vaccination card, and to never carry them on you in case you do become a victim of theft or pick-pocketing.

title: Definitely something to experience
text: Really a great place for architecture and history. We had a guide helped immensely. We visited numerous place. Be prepared for a work out as the streets are quite hilly

title: Spectacular City
text: We had a lovely tour with our guide around all the sights and sounds. The parade  at the Basilica was amazing.

title: A fun day at the Artisanal Market
text: We had a free day waiting for our covid test results before flying to the Galapagos Islands for a (fantastic!) cruise on LaPinta.  We walked there from the JWMarriott (very nice) & shopped for an hour or so, not even covering a fraction of the market (we had 3 young boys in our group).  There were some beautiful items for sale at very reasonable prices.  We felt very safe walking as a group of 8 back & forth from the hotel.

title: Loved it!
text: I love Quito and do love the old city area of Quito. Lots of history and beautiful architecture. Whenever I visit Quito, I always stay in the old district.

title: Dirty and unsafe
text: Be prepared for lots of graffiti, stray dogs, homeless people and poop on the sidewalks.Also, everything is basically closed all the time.And apparently it’s not safe to go there at night.I guess it’s the best part of Quito but not comparable to other cities and nothing spectacular.

title: Historic city
text: We had a great guide in Washington sierra. Hope you are able to meet him, we found his contact info on a forum in trip advisor. He is a retired Spanish teacher, drives taxi, a great tourist guide who speaks English. He took us all around Quito. We enjoyed the views and architecture. We had good weather but it fogs up on and off. Definitely check out the domes and church

title: My favorite party of Quito
text: To be honest I didn't think I was going to like this area as much as I did.  So many things to see, so much going on.  I highly recommend a guide to give you the most information.

title: Great place to stay and visit all the historical sites
text: We spent 4 nights at the Casa Montero right across the street from the Santo Domingo church and had a wonderful experience. We were able to walk to most anywhere in Old Town including La Ronda street where the food was great, Presidential Square, San Francisco Church, National Biscilica, among many other amazing sites. It was a busy time and we enjoyed the dancing and music from our room due to the celebration activities leading up to today which is the Foundation of Quito which is a local holiday but there was a lot of pre-celebrations each night.

title: Historical Muslim (moorish) and Spanish architecture
text: A nice stroll through the old town displaying Muslim and Spanish architecture, especially windows and gates/doors of the buildings. Cobble streets add to the environment. Wish it would be considered safe walking alone. Felt a certain tense even with the guide. Armed guards at stores do not provide relief

title: nice historic center
text: walked around the old town on sat and sun, plenty to see and felt safe with police at almost every corner.  saw  some street performers doing Andean dances which was very entertaining. don't be cheap make sure to tip them!

title: Old Town Quito
text: This is a vibrant area, with so much history, plenty of shops and tons of food options! There are numerous hotels and hostels here. Some of the area gets a bad rep as "dangerous," but I didn't have any problems during the week I was in town. Very close to everything!

title: Quito old town by foot
text: Very interesting walk through the old city of Quito. Several old office and residential buildings to see and the walk to the Basilica involves some very steep roads. But worth the walk!

title: Worth a few days
text: My daughter (13) and I spent 3 days in Centro Historico. It is a lovely old town -- it's like an elderly woman whose beautiful skirt is slightly frayed at the edges. Many police patrol the area, which is good, as pickpockets are frequent in Quito during these post-pandemic times.

title: Really Nice Old Town!
text: I am not a town lover, but this was very nice and quite easy to find my way around. I walked around on my first day in Quito, I didn’t take a tour, which I probably should have, I would if I came again.

title: Safe if you are sensible.
text: I had read a few reviews here and elsewhere that the Old Town area is not safe. Our hotel was located in Old Town and my wife and I were out walking both during the day and early afternoon. We felt perfectly safe as there were always a lot of people around and law enforcement was visible especially near the Plaza de la Independencia. If you don’t flash cash and take precautions like keeping phones and wallets in front pockets, you should be fine. Get out and walk and enjoy this UNESCO World Heritage Site.

title: Dangerous day or night
text: We are world travelers in our 50’s.  My husband is tall, fit and very muscular.   However, in the very same area, in old town, as we were approaching the La Ronda area at 2 o’clock in the afternoon,  my husband was approached by a very dark skinned man.  I immediately sensed danger, turned to head the other way while yelling for my husband to follow. When I looked back, my husband was fighting off the man who had a knife in his hand.  I was yelling bloody murder to the top of my lungs nonstop.  There were only a few people in the vicinity but no one came to help.   When my husband had the guy pinned on the ground, I ran to help only to be greeted by a second man coming after me.  He noticed that my husband had the upper hand, and turned on him with a metal pipe in hand.  Like many of you,  I too agree this can happen anywhere.  But in broad daylight with people around?  Luckily, we both escaped uninjured.  And for those of you that will say~give him the bag and don’t fight back, in hindsight we realized that my husband’s actions saved me from being attacked by the other man.

title: Really nice
text: I love old Quito .  I feel it is the nicest colonial city in the Americas.  Mexico City's plaza is also wonderful I feel safe as there was lots of police in   The plaza.  last time I visited I also felt safe but we know to be cautious esp during these Covid times

title: Cute area, but watch your bag!
text: This is the tourist center of Quito. There's lots of stores, cafes, restaurants, churches, and museums to spend your time. However, this is also a place where tourists get robbed, so don't flash your iPhone or wallet and avoid going here alone at night.

title: Beautiful and Underrated!
text: We really didn't know what to expect from Quito in general, and we ended up absolutely loving it! The narrow cobble streets, the gorgeous churches, the Plazas, and the atmosphere were just terrific!

title: Beautiful colonial architecture
text: Walking the streets of Quito is fun and historically quite interesting. Also... very nice museum related to the pre-colombian civilizations

title: Best part of Quito
text: Walking around old town was probably the highlight of Quito. Not much open - Covid - but was still a really cool experience and can only imagine what it would be like during non-Covid times 👌

title: Walking tour
text: The old town is beautiful and best to visit during the daytime has it can be a bit dangerous in the evening

title: Beautiful Old Town
text: We had a wonderful walking tour through the Old Town - an UNESCO Heritage Site.  The architecture and numerous churches are amazing to explore. The streets are clean and the people friendly.  Lots of beautiful buildings, shops and restaurants. We were told to avoid at night right now.

title: A Concrete Jungle
text: Beautiful place to go. Wonderfull places and hostess. People in there are amazing, there are a lot of activities you can do and thousands of pictures to take.

title: Walking by nignt
text: That is the best experience, as all churches and its towers are lighted, a beautiful scene, also there is  few people on the streets so you can make wonderful photos

title: Allow yourself a day or two to accommodate to the high altitude
text: We met-up with good friends in Quito, the capital of Ecuador.  This beautiful colonial city is filled with wonderful architecture, churches, plazas, shops, restaurants and it is the greatest historic center in South America.  Allow yourself a day or two to accommodate to the high altitude.

title: The Heart of Quito, Colonial but Uniquely Ecuadorian
text: A UNESCO World Heritage Site - one of the first - this is the best preserved - and oldest, extant colonial city remaining in South and Central America. It is a living history - once a symbol of European hegemony over indigenous people, it now is truly Ecuadorian - mixing the colonial style with art, music, life that is distinctly modern, but unique. The architecture is incredible, and many neat little shops. Can wander around for a day or two - be sure to visit the Presidential Plaza - a central focal point of life in Quito, where locals spontaneously show up with instruments, or even microphones to sing - the life here is incredible, it is beautiful at night and most parts are well policed and safe. Avoid the edges of Old Town unless you are used to traveling in developing countries and know how to keep an eye out for potential pickpockets. Stay in the core, and you'll find it a wonderful place that may make you want to never leave - you will never find this in the US.

title: Lovely colonial buildings
text: really enjoyable to stroll around the streets of the old Quito to view all the old colonial buildings and architecture.

Quito is a beautiful city and also the one that saw me born. It has several tourist places to visit such as the Carolina park, which is a place for strolling and tranquility, a clean and well-preserved public park, well-cared for plants and landscaping and well-respected. Another place is the bun, where you can see the landmarks, its morphology and fabric of this entire colonial city, it is a gem. The Quito cable car, we buy tickets at the box office for $ 7 each, each cabin fits 6 people so boarding is relatively fast, the climb is impressive, in just 15 minutes you climb from the 3117 meters high in the start at 3945 m. Once up there is a cafeteria with impressive views and a beautiful viewpoint called de los Volcanes, because if the day is clear you can see a few of those that surround the city of Quito. Another place that is worth visiting is half the world, a place designed to attract tourists, with a supposed line that indicates half the world, it has a good infrastructure throughout the walk. It really exceeds our expectations, the $ 5.00 entrance fee is well worth it, the great museums, the shops, the train tour, the monuments, the food and the people make it a beautiful experience to get to know. Highly recommendedQuito has its very old streets, that is to say, colonial streets that are worth visiting and enjoying the atmosphere that it gives us, recommended to visit and walk with the family.

title: Quito "Plaza foch"
text: Quito is a very large city, it has a very specific architectural legacy, that is why it is a cultural heritage of humanity a beautiful and very known tourist place of Quito is the "Plaza Foch", it is located between Mariscal Foch and Reina Victoria, the neighbors state that its official name is Plaza del Quinde, to the modern metal sculpture installed there.It is a very beautiful place for national and foreign tourism. Around it there are many commercial premises, bars, restaurants, hotels and clubs at night. If you go on holidays, go through Quito, it is a beautiful city, there are many places to visit.

title: Beautiful
text: History and architecture is gorgeous! Never gets old visiting Old Town! Plan on walking a lot, but worth it.

title: Part of our excursion was to walk through the Old Town
text: We had a nice, long walk through the Old Town. It was extremely hot & humid in late February, but did not rain. Many beautiful building to feast our eyes upon. Love the architecture. Love the park place. Unique area.

title: Lots of Beautiful Architecture
text: There are so many beautiful buildings (churches, governmental buildings, etc) here. Popular with the locals as well. Enjoyed myself walking the streets and admire the architecture.

title: Beautiful Square
text: We were in Old Town about a month after the riots.  There was still a lot of fencing and razor wire on some of the buildings, but the Square was still beautiful and bustling with locals out on a sunny day enjoying ice cream and fresh Andean air.

title: Great!
text: It is beautiful city to walk around. Very safe.  The architecture of the building is gorgeous.  You will find different restaurants, stores and churches that you can enjoy. Great for pictures!

title: Compact historic center
text: Well preserved old historic center with rich colonial architecture where all the sites are easy to reach just walking around. Very safe due to heavy presence of the police but of course general precaution is advice.  Many cafes and restaurant to sit down and watch the world pass. The community hostel offers daily free guided tour.

title: An exceptionnaly beautiful old town
text: Quito Old Town is exceptionnaly beautiful with ist numerous churches, narrow streets, picturesque squares, amazing views. It's an incredible pleasure discovering it by foot.

title: Well preserved historic district
text: Quito’s old town is a must see while in Quito! It is filled with awe inspiring churches, quaint restaurants, well maintained parks, cozy cafes and so much more. Great place to stay while in Quito as there are also hotels and hostels a plenty. If you are not staying you can make it a full day excursion. Also look out for free walking tours if your interested in learning more.

title: Lovely
text: Interesting and easy to walk around. Lovely people. Feels safe in Old Town. Great churches and museums.

title: You cannot visit Quito and not come to the Old Town!
text: This is absolutely a must-do if you do nothing else in Quito. Full of things to see and do and visit (just take a look online). Easy to walk around for a few hours by yourselves with a little map and see all of the Governmental and Religious buildings. The buildings are a wonderful mix of colonial and architectural styles through the ages. The back drop against the mountains is stunning. Pop into Pacari shop and pick up some local Ecuadorian chocolate - YUM.

title: Bucket list!  This is a "must see" historic center of the city--compared to all others
text: Bucket list!  This is a "must see" historic center of the city--compared to all others

title: Came for the Sunday of Carnival
text: Got swept up in the parades and excitement of this family friendly expression of Carnival. Be prepared to get sprayed with aerosol foam.

title: The Grand Plaza centers a compact history of colonial Quito.
text: Old town in Quito is one of the most compact and well represented colonial examples I've seen in either Colombia or Ecuador. The center requires only a couple of days to take in just about everything in the tour books, yet Quito has so much more to offer throughout the city and nearby volcanic regions.

title: Interesting
text: We booked a City Tour with lunch, excellent value at $35. We enjoyed a walking tour through this fascinating City calling in at the main sights and squares. La Ronda is particularly pretty.

title: Great walking in Old Town
text: We had a wonderful day walking in Panama’s Old Town. The buildings are beautiful with many balconies lined with flowers. There was a very quaint area Covered with flowering trees near the water front where local art and craft vendors were selling their wares. A beautiful area to get photos of downtown Panama!  Take Time and enjoy walking the area with shopping and great places to eat.

Great to walk in Quito Old Town. Lots of shops, cafes, restaurants and seeing the center with the architecture, plazas and churches is great fun. Slight warning, a friend had his wallet stolen so be careful and pay attention. He got it back though by punching the guy in the face. Not sure that is recommended but you know.

title: Beautiful Architecture.
text: Super cool buildings and the whole area is easily walkable.  We felt very safe the entire time, and there is a consistent presence of tourism police.  We wandered the narrow cobbled streets, took in the architecture, visited Plaza Grande and ate in a nice little restaurant.  We also encountered an amusing (temporary) downpour which added to the experience!

title: Quito old town
text: Great historic centre..wide open plazas..interesting old buildings inc churches..Ronda area good nightlifeJust sit in plaza grande and watch all of Quito go by..good coffee..nearby is the Claro shop..where english speaking can put a sim in your phone with plenty of gigs for internet for 10$Nightlife not so hotGreat city

title: A rich and beautiful colonial city full of majesty
text: A lovely colonial city alive and vibrant until the early evening and then all at once falling asleep and rope. Beyond the boundaries of the Old City it is not safe to walk around in the dark. The buildings and arrangement of the Old City convey a feeling of a good old good movie

title: Lovely stroll thru Old Town
text: We scheduled a half day tour of old town. This is a must do.The building, churches and history is wonderful.Our knowledgeable guide, Paul, was great with stories, history, recommendations and places to stop.A must do.

title: Tours by Locals all day tour
text: We had an excellent all day tour with Tours by Locals. Our guide spoke excellent English. He picked us up at our hotel and drove us to many places, though a lot of the tour was walking around in certain areas. He knew the history of the Quito old town and took us to interesting places, all of which he told us about. I would definitely recommend Tours by Locals and touring Quito Old Town.

title: A really nice place to be
text: The reason all the guidebooks say to walk through Old Town is because you really have to do it.  Just follow their advice and you can't go wrong

title: A step back in time
text: We had a tour guide to take us to the various historical sites in the area. It’s an easy walk with lots of shops and restaurants.

title: Old town
text: World heritage. Quito is beautiful city and also unsafe in some areas. I recommend minimum 3 days for Old town.

title: Beautiful World Heritage Site!
text: The history and charm of Old Town are an amazing way to spend a day or three wandering around. Wander into churches, taste the great chocolate made in Ecuador and stop to sit and enjoy the plazas. With that said, I want to put out a traveler's warning: my husband had his wallet stolen within the first 20 minutes we were there. Here's how it happened: we were walking/standing on the street looking around when all of a sudden it seemed we had bird poop all over us.  Immediately 2 guys came up and offered to "help us".  They had napkins to wipe it off.  As they were "helping" us, one guy lifted my husband's wallet out of his (front) pants pocket. We didn't know this happened until about a half hour later and by then they had charged nearly $5000 USD on the card.  Beware of professional pick-pocketers out there!  Don't let this alter your plans about going here, just be super careful and vigilant about anyone crowding your space. Wear a money belt or under clothing bag.
There is good reason that this is a UNESCO World Heritage Sight. Colorful, well persevered colonial style with beautiful churches/convents around every corner

title: Very interesting
text: City famed for its high vantage point in the Andean mountains and its ancient Incan significance. Narrow streets, beautiful buildings and plazas. So much interesting history.

title: A must see!
text: Extremely interesting and beautiful. We took a 3 hour walking tour that was well paced and informative. If you are in Quito save a day for the Old town. Sit in the square and people watch, have coffee and breathe in the experience.
Great city so many sites highly recommend a visit.  3-4 days would exhaust you by sights sounds tastes and smells.

title: So much to see
text: and now so much to walk, they change some street to be pedestrian only, it make so much sense to enjoy of a nice walk, stooping on the middle of the street an looking one of kind details on the surroundings

title: Well worth the visit - plan a whole day
text: there is so much to see in the colonial city center.  Many of these buildings have courtyards so go exploring.  Don't miss any of the churches; they are all amazing.

title: Must see
text: This is an UNESCO Heritage Site and the prime destination of any visit to Quito.  Here you will find the many churches, plazas, some museums, restaurants and other interesting sites. It is well preserved and just wandering its streets is a pleasant experience. It is safe, clean and very well-policed.  Please note that it is hilly and in some places very steep but Uber is inexpensive and helps out. Though all the modern hotels are outside this district, there are ample smaller ones that are very charming and some inexpensive ones. I believe that 3/4 of all interesting sites of Quito are located here.

title: Multi-family Trip to Heaven :) With Gate 1 Travel and Wilo our Main Guy <3
text: Getting there! Our group of five families made a scene together on the way to Quito, capital of Ecuador.  On average, we spent 5 hours of each day on the bus except one day in Amazon. The main benefit of having a large group was having our own bus. We had a great time! The roads in Ecuador were nice, lighted and clean, many stretch of the road similar to roads heading to North in Iran! We went from altitude of 3000 to 10,000 to 600 in our travels. A few got altitude sickness but nothing stopped the show! The icing on the cake was our main guide Wilson (Wilo) Tituana fromGate 1 Travel. For the 8 nights and days, he cared for every aspect of our trip, he made sure we are all safe, he took us to fun places some of which was not part of our formal itinerary and he felt like a big brother who kindly shared his vast knowledge of Ecuador's many aspects with us. I feel like I have a good friend. Thank you Wilo for doing such an amazing job!Day 2- Visiting Colonial Quito and walk through its cobblestone streets, the 7 tons golden Basilica and the ice cream making on the way to the Middle of the World!Ecuator! Where you have one foot in Southern Hemisphere and one on the North! Very nice complex with various exhibits, nice shops and nicer coffee shop! We had lunch on our own and did I mention the coffee was really good?!Wilo bought a huge bunch of roses and he made kids and hubbies to give it to the ladies! Roses are one of the main exports for Ecuador! I don’t see roses as big and full here in the U.S.

title: Just walk and enjoy
text: Just walk, enjoy Quito's skies, Quito's architecture and the local scenery of local bureaucrats, merchants, priests, nuns, tour guides and tourists all creating one community as a whole for a moment.

title: Tourist
text: Good start to trip take Quito Tour on and off busWalking around to each church and good people watching

title: Enjoyed walking around!
text: We loved walking around old town Quito together! We went with a tour on a bus and found we enjoyed being able to get out and walk around and explore what we wanted. Great activity for couples.

title: Fun Place to visit
text: It was nice to walk around but you probably should do this during the day and avoid night strolls.  Many warnings from locals about pickpockets.

title: Fantastic!
text: Even though the Old Town can be hilly in areas it is very walkable. Around every corner is another unique experience. The city is alive during the day but quickly shuts down when it gets dark. Plan your large meal for lunch as many of the restaurants shut down in the evening and it can be hard to find a place to eat dinner. The museums are great, the architecture is great and the people are fantastic. Plenty of sites to see.

title: Average
text: Nice old area, nothing spectacular. Police present appreciated - no criminal activity noticed thou. In front of the Palacio a few demonstrators shouting in Spanish with the swastika sign attached to their arms.

title: Soooo beautiful
text: Spent the morning wondering through this beautiful area. Sights, sounds, smells, people......so much to see and do. The churches were stunning. I could have spent all day but alas so much to do and so little time

title: Plenty of Tourists
text: We enjoyed walking through Old Town and visiting the plazas, churches and museums. There is a large police presence which I find unsettling. The first hour we were there, we were squirted with a grey substance and immediately an elderly fellow appeared with napkins to help us clean it off. I knew immediately it was a scam and sharply told the guy no. Be careful in this area of town. Was also surprised by the number of prostitutes hanging around. Despite these negatives it is an appealing area for pedestrians and sightseeing. Don’t take anything of value, have a wallet that tucks into your pants from your belt, and carry everything in front of you. Lovely but lots of petty theft it seems.

title: Amo Quito, but... beware...
text: I love Quito.  I've made three visits and will go again.  There's just so much to explore.  There are all sorts of interesting shops and wonderful food.  Attractions like the Basilica and the numerous museums are not to be missed.With that said, travelers be careful.  I am a solo guy that got set on by a group of prostitutes around 6PM, plenty of day light, on a reasonably busy street that I thought was safe, even though I saw the hookers.As they started some grasping, one snatched an iphone from my pocket.  I knew instantly, and ultimately got it back after making a big scene.Still, I'll go much more aware and much more careful next time  Situational awareness... though Quito is wonderful, don't leave home without it.

title: Nice old town
text: A very pleasant location. A lot of shops to buy goods on reasonable prices.Unfotunately you can not walk up to the Panechillo, because its too dangerous to get robbed!🙈

title: Lovely walking streets to explore
text: It is fun to just go out and walk in a different direction in this old town.  Lots to explore with local stores, street vendors, museums and churches.

title: Best-preserved historic center of Latin America
text: The old town was founded in 1534 by Spanish settlers on the ruins of an Inca town. 200 years later urban architect Dionisio Alcedo y Herrera designed the original city plan, which is still intact until this day. At the time a modern plan with the pattern of a chessboard, but fitting the slopes of nearby Pichincha volcano and Itchimbia and El Panecillo hills.Not only the old colonial structures are still there, but also traditional crafts and know-how developed over centuries are preserved by the people who have been living in the old town for generations. Another reason why the old town is on the UNESCO world heritage list is the influence of the Baroque school of Quito which can be observed in the many Spanish style churches and institutions. Especially the interiors of the Church and Jesuit College of La Compañía is staggering.Cars and buses were not planned to take space in the old town. Hope that the authorities will eventually take motorized vehicles out. Now only on Sunday mornings there is no black smoke and noise, as only pedestrians and cyclists are allowed in most of the old town. Wonderful to walk around in fresh air.Keep in mind that the old town is in the 'poor' area of Quito and after 9PM almost deserted. The modern town is towards the north.

title: Old Town Quito Sightseeing and Food Walking Tour with Pablo
text: What a great tour we booked via Viator! Pablo met my family of four, with our two young adult daughters, in the Plaza Grande at the statue and took us on a nearly five-hour walking tour. He was very thorough in explaining all to us. We stopped along the way to learn about various buildings, churches, eats, and all. We spent time in a chocolate shop to learn the process and taste various sweets. We learned so much about Quito. What an exciting tour!

title: Picturesque and pleasant
text: We stayed overnight at the Hotel San Francisco in the Old Town and were very impressed by both.  Excellent architecture and friendly people, oh, and wonderful coffee!

title: Beautiful, old architecture with wonderful history
text: It's a interesting, photogenic part of town. It's absolutely a must-see, but be prepared for people selling their wares to approach you constantly. Unless you want to go home with a ton of trinkets, be prepared to kindly say no and even be a little stern. Now, that said, the people are trying to make a living and feed their family, so do try to be kind.We went to a number of churches, wandered a bit and had a lovely lunch that was our best meal of the trip, in Old Town. You could spend days exploring.

title: Nice stroll but lack of English
text: Was a nice stroll around the old town visiting various museums. Lots of police presence which made me feel quite safe, but the lack of English by various tourist attractions was disappointing.

title: Narrow walkways, fascinating architecture, plazas, and people!
text: Narrow walkways, fascinating architecture, plazas, and people! This is a living historical site with shops, restaurants and people simply doing what they have been doing for hundreds of years. Streets and roadways are full of irregular steps, be wary, it’s part of the areas charm.

title: Quito's Plaza de la Independencia/Plaza Grande
text: Heartbeat of Centro Historica/Old Town.Centered by the Monument of the Independence Heroes, and flanked by the Presidential Palace, the Archbishop's Palace, Municipal Hall, and Catedral Metropolitana de Quito.Definitely a 'bucket list visit when in Quito.

title: History abound
text: We went on the double decker hop on hop off bus so we could have information on our surroundings.  The churches we saw were amazing. We checked out the Basilic church, the San Francisco and Compania De Jesus (gold church). There is so much to see I was using up all my storage on my phone. The Mirador El Panecillo was not only beautiful itself but the view from there was amazing.

title: Interesting quarter
text: You can easily visit this quarter either staying locally at one of the Hostals (inexpensive) or hotels or take one of the bus lines to this area. Buses are very cheap US 25 cents for adults.  Wise to run the three main networks from one end of the city to another in non peak times. It is so cheap and you can visit parks or sites and for another quarter jump on again. Best deal around and way to get an overview.

title: Worth a visit
text: Enjoyed a free walking tour. Surrounded by hills and high altitude. The tour ended in a chocolate place where they tried to sell us stuff.

title: Recommend a local guide
text: There is a lot to do in old Town but it's mostly just looking at architecture unless you get a guide to give you the stories that go with the buildings and plazas, etc.  I walked it on my own due to some time considerations and don't feel like I appreciated it enough.  Next time I come to Quito I'll probably make a point to stay in Old Town and see a little more.

title: A lot to see & do here
text: This is a fascinating place.  There are several historical sites to visit, plus the people watching is great.

title: Feels like a modern city
text: We traveled throughout the Andes and into the Amazon before ending our trip in Quito. After all that, which felt like a second-world country, this was quite a refreshing step back to modern times: big city, lots of things to see and do, but of course still in Ecuador. Very enjoyable visit, wish we could've spent more time exploring the city and surroundings!

title: Historic part of Quito
text: Lots of museaums. There is the cathedral of 3 tons of gold. Rich in history. The entrance fees vary from 2 to 5 usd.

title: Beautiful architecture
text: My visit was brief but there are so many beautiful buildings. This area gives off a real feel of the city. A lot of locals in the area.

title: Never disappointed with Quito
text: Walking around Quito is always a great way to "get into the mood" of being back in Ecuador. Staying at a great hotel near all the action helps too ( Travellers Inn)

title: Big expectations met
text: The old town of Quito is truly a must see in Latin America.Unbelievable the will of the people who created this place.Design with the purpose to foster religious ideas.It is a living museum where with a little bit of imagination one can travel back to the days of the conquistadors

title: Must see the old town
text: We enjoyed strolling through the old town. Husband got a shoe shine, bought chocolate, and lingered over excellent cups of coffee around the square.

title: Lots of sights to see
text: We had a great time walking around old town.  Lots of nice plazas, great restaurants, beautiful churches, colonial buildings, and very good people watching.  I highly recommend bringing lots of small bills and $1 coins as many places would not or could not change any bill higher than $10. You will need those $1 coins!  My $20 bills were pretty useless except for at higher end restaurants and shops.

title: A town full of history
text: I was going to rate Quito old Town with four stars, until we say the changing of the guards on Monday morning at 9. Spectacular. Quito old Town is beautiful and full of history, we did the walking tour and saw and heard most of it. The city is very busy and there are many people selling stuff on the streets. It is advisable to watch your belongings closely, though there are many police on the streets. In La Ronda you can find many restaurants that offer live music, they charge a "cover" for the music though. I found the old Town t o be very save.

title: Colonial architecture and pleasant ambience
text: We got a taxi to the Old Town and wandered round the different squares, stopping for souvenirs and refreshments.There were lovely views in all directions, the birds were singing and we were pleasantly surprised at the ambience there, considering that there had been strikes only a couple of weeks beforehand.

title: Memorable historic heritage site
text: I was told not to miss a visit to the Quito Old Town, and I agree with this advice. Although there were a lot of street “vendors”(people selling anything and everything for a little money everywhere, and some of these individuals were quite aggressive), the area itself was beautiful. Be mindful of the altitude.

title: A serenity within silence
text: Having visited the old town just after the recent burnings & demonstrations in 2019, it was quite surprising to see everyone back to business as usual, the atmosphere was charming and still very welcoming, the nite lighting of the old town creates a picture perfect postcard with a certain feeling of calmness. The cool breeze with the light raindrops was quite perfect in many ways. The architecture of the old town was something to take away forever. A place one could visit and marvel at what it was in the old days, what it still is to many people today and what it holds to most of the locals.

title: Too many people
text: Old town Quito is historic and has some beautiful buildings. However, the traffic is really bad and there are just a few too many people to make it enjoyable. If you are visiting Quito, there are definitely some sites worth seeing in old town, however the neighborhood, in and of itself, is just okay.

title: Very cool place to wander
text: Spent parts of three days wandering around Old Town.  Wonderful place to find food, take pictures, see a wild collection of Christmas lights and spot interesting people.  Even in a pouring rain, the area was full of life and busy.  Went shopping for art to bring home and had a great time.  This is one of my favorite places on the planet.

title: Peep into history
text: Old town is the heart of Quito. If you are not used to the altitude, do mark where you want to go as you will get tired. Otherwise, just wander around and you will get anywhere. Ease yourself, have plenty of time and follow the locals for good food and bargains.

title: Both charming and fascinating
text: This World Heritage Sight offers beautiful churches, interesting shopping, including friendly street vendors, and some very good restaurants.  Great for photos!

title: Giant experience
text: Fantastic downtown with numerous places to visit. No wonder that it belongs to the Unesco Worldheritage with all its churches, museums and the general atmosphere. We felt secure and enjoyed the town highly.

title: For walkers
text: We made two walking forays, one with a guide then on our own. A student guide gave us a whirlwind of the Basilica (thank you Adrian). Did other churches, caught a little concert in Sagrario. Meandered into Museo Egas, etc. Don’t miss Chez Tiff chocolate. Parque la Alameda, very nice inexpensive lunch just across at Fruterria Monserrate convenient to ATM, etc. Walk and you will discover.

title: Warm and relaxed
text: Quito is a very busy and noisy city. So walking through old town where there is less traffic is a relaxing experience. Tip: take the free walking tour at the CarpeDM hostel.

title: Must go: UNESCO site
text: There area number of really interesting buildings in the Old Town but a wander around absorbing the atmosphere and architecture is well worthwhile.  It is quite a small area so readily explored
Prepare to spend one full day in the Quito Old Town. Very well preserved, organized and clean. Stop in every corner to visit each church.

title: Mesmerising town
text: We stayed just a couple of minutes away fr the Old town so we're always there to see the grandiose churches and the old gov't buildings. Weekend is most special coz there are dancers in the middle of the street. Also the the red light is up, there are some entertainers like jugglers, magician, dancers etc to entertain the people stuck in the traffic. There are also lots of restaurant and convenient store around so we won't go hungry. I heard some says we have to be careful w/ our stuff but i found people of Ecuador to be generally nice and welcome-y.

title: Beautiful city but sadly dangerous at night!
text: The city is beautiful - from the basilica to downtown. However, I do want to mention how dangerous it is to walk around Quito at night. My boyfriend and I went to the main area (Ronda) and it seemed fairly safe as there was lots of people. We then thought we would just walk a couple of blocks back to the hotel but got attacked by three men. Thankfully nothing too serious happened and we were able to run away with only a few bruises and a black eye but if you are planning on staying here please use a taxi going to restaurants and back once it’s dark. An interesting fact is that none of the taxi drivers or people we met seemed shocked that this had happened to us but rather accepted it as a normal event. Perhaps the perception of what is normal or dangerous differs.

title: Walk the entire zone
text: A step back in time.  Beautiful old buildings.  Very nice locals.  You can buy all your gifts as well as hardware, dishwashers, the whole lot!

title: Not as exspected
text: I thaught there would be old renovated houses, and cozy streets. No, just the main buildings by the plaza, and not much places where you could walk elsewhere looking. But the plaza is nice, and the buildings are ok. The golden church is amazing. But that was it .

title: Nice city except for the numerous pickpockets
text: Plenty to see and do for a few days but many tourists had issues with pickpockets (mustard sprayed on them by well dressed person).

title: Nice area
text: Good place to walk around  browse shops, eat and visit the local sites. Only went during the day. Would go back

title: Fascinating area, full of great sights and packed with people
text: This is the obvious place to start a visit to Quito, with some stunning buildings - among them the Basilica and the gold-covered interior of the Iglesia de la Compania - some great squares, particularly the Plaza San Francisco and of course the hugely-popular Plaza Grande, a couple of fascinating places to visit such as the excellent and highly-informative Museo de la Ciudad, and the traditional street of old houses La Ronda. There are good eateries including some fine little cafes on the southern side of Plaza Grande (try the Dulceria Colonia). Most people are really friendly, but do watch out for a well-known street robbery trick involving a pair of people one of whom squirts or drops a liquid on your hair, which one pretends to helpfully clean off while the other makes off with your bag, purse or wallet. Sadly it happened to three people we know in the first couple of hours in the city, and it's the only real blot on what's a lovely and fascinating city to visit. The Old Town is busy but easy to walk around, with the streets all laid out in a grid pattern and most of them now pedestrianised. Great fun, but take care.

title: Wonderful old town
text: This is a superb city to walk around and there are so many churches, museums and plazas. A spectacular capital city it's worth spending a few days wandering around

title: Sunday at leisure
text: We visited old town Quito on a late Sunday morning. The locals were milling around and older gentlemen, dressed in their Sunday finest sat on benches. The central plaza was a good place to capture photographs of a variety of people and vendors. Our tour guide frequently cautioned us to be wary of pickpockets. Being relatively crowded it was easy to see how a pickpocket could ply his trade and slip away. Not far away is the basilica and museum of San Francisco. As mass was in progress we took the back steps up to the choir loft to gaze upon one of the most ornately designed wall behind the altar I have ever seen.Well worth the visit.

title: Unbelieveably preserved look at centuries old architecture...
text: Beautiful and scenic with narrow streets brimming with history.  It is well worth a visit and is the kind of place where you can get those once in lifetime photos.

title: One of the best old town centres in the world
text: Great experience to walk around in central Quito, with its many squares, museums, churches, shops and restaurants. You can stay here for days without getting bored.

title: Beginning Ecuador pacific coast vacation!
