text: Beautiful, quiet, relaxed!! Don’t miss it ! This is where it all began, city in the hill. Tour with quite started our perfect experience! Frontier!!

title: 'Old town is a cultural melting pot'
text: The oldest capital city in South America is a cultural melting pot of museums, artesanal shops traditional trades people, local dancing and music along with the Cathedral Merced and Compania, A visit to the old town and its steeeped streets is a must for anyone who loves history and in particular that of South America.

title: A great free walk
text: A lovely place to visit for old world charm and history! I happened to be there during a parade which was an added bonus.

title: Old Town walking tour
text: We did this with the Free Walking Tour folks.  Along with us was a guy from Austria and a guy from Amsterdam. We walked up to the plaza from the nearby bus stop, then wandered the area for a few hours.  Thanks to our guide we got a good feel for the area and later on went back on our own.  The churches and museums were stunning.  The plaza was full of people enjoying the wonderful weather.

title: Atmosphere Galore.
text: Strolling through the old town is the highlight of a Quito visit. Take your time, sit on the straps of the grand plazas and watch the street life. Food sellers, live music, beggars, and cafes. Drop into churches and museums. A delightful half to whole day activity.

title: Away from the hurly burly of mainstream Quito this srea is an oasis.v
text: The main square is bordered by a very welcoming tourist information office. There is also a row of small cafes doing good coffee and snacks. The cathedral dominates a side of the area and is well worth a visit. Entry is 4 usd. There is seating in the square and maintains the grandeur of its colonial roots.

title: Worth a visit
text: I loved the old town, lots of lovely places to eat and drink.  Although opening hours vary a lot so one shop that maybe closed for most of your holiday will suddenly be open another day.  Not the best place to shop in the world but shop around as two shops selling the same thing could vary in price by as much as double!  If you only have one day in Quito try to make it a Sunday where the streets come alive with performers and market stalls.

title: Quaint and historic part of town
text: Fortunately the recent protests against the lifting of subsidies hasn’t impacted the old town. It’s size is such that you can see the majority during a stopover. There’s plenty of history which our guide covered well.

title: Wonderful Place--Take Altitude Pills before if you don't do regular cardio exercises
text: Quito isn't as high as Machu Picchu but I suffered altitude sickness for 2 days.  There are so many winding streets to explore so don't lose any time.Lots of cobblestone and narrow sidewalks so wear very practical shoes.  Drink lots of water too, it helps with the altitude.Saturdays, they close off many streets for pedestrian-only traffic and if you are a little more daring, on Sunday they close off even more for cycling and you can rent a bike.  Just remember, there are lots of inclines to pedal so it helps to be in shape.

title: Colonial Architecture
text: It's good to walk around the streets and squares at your leisure, soaking up the atmosphere. Lots of well-preserved Spanish architecture, and plenty to see and do. We spent a whole day walking by ourselves.

title: Beware the pseudo pigeons
text: Nice square but beware of the pick pockets. Walking through the streets we thought that a pigeon had done the dirty on us. A man rushed up offering to help clean up the mess, soon his buddy was in on the act. It's all a con to let them frisk you down and attempt to steal your wallet. Mine was in a zipped pocket so they got nothing but we heard of others who weren't so lucky. It cost us a cleaning bill and judging by the volume the pigeon must have been the size of an ostrich.

title: Beware Pickpockets
text: Warning: There is a gang operating who spray passing tourists with what seems like bird excrement.  They then come up to you with tissues offering to help clean you up.  This is an excuse to  feel for any valuables on you and access to your bags.  If this happens to you, don't let them come near you.  We were targetted twice but fortunately nothing was taken.  On the positive side, on the first occassion several locals came to our assistance and chased the pickpockets away.

title: Magnificent Colonial Architecture.
text: The reason why Quito's Old Town is listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site is that it is an extremely well-preserved example of Spanish colonial architecture. Within the vast expanse of the "Centro Historico" almost all the buildings are from the 17th, 18th or 19th centuries - there is almost nothing from the 20th century - which means that a stroll through this area is highly authentic. Another feature of Quito's Old Town is the preponderance of religious sites - churches and nunneries - devoted to female religious figures. Indeed the patron saint of Quito is Saint Mariana, who expressed her devotion to Christianity by self-flagellation and self-mutilation - there can be no more profound statement of individual faith and purity of spirit. Apart from the historical aspects, Ecuadorians are extremely friendly and welcoming. This alone is a reason to visit the country.

title: Wonderful, Bustling Place
text: There is so much to see and do in Old Town Quito that you would have trouble ever being bored. We felt perfectly safe while we walked the streets even in the evening (this was just before the austerity measures were started), though the hills and altitude made it difficult for the first day or two.

title: We had a guide...need one for the history and info!
text: Wow - 60 churches in Old Town....you must go into a couple of these...they are mind-glowingly beautiful...so much gold and ornate carvings!

title: Must do
text: With its many amazing churches, narrow streets, the small park at the main square and lots of pedestrian areas Quito Old Town is a historical, pittoresque and lovely neighbourhood not to be missed when visiting the Ecuadorian capital. There are many options for having coffee /a snack as well. Beautiful during the day and at early evening when the buildings are lit.

title: One of the most beautiful places in South America
text: Quito's Centro is charming.  Filled with churches and colonial-era buildings, but is a living City Center, not a tourist museum.  Some of the streets are pedestrian malls.  Great area for walking, shopping, exploring.  Safe.

title: The place
text: We stayed here, shopped here, walked here and ate here. It is why you go to Quito. There are a number of pedestrian only streets.

title: One of Latin America’s Great Old Towns
text: To visit Quito and not visit it’s Central Square and Old Town is simply absurd. Ideally both should be visited on a sunny day and a chilly evening.

title: Charming
text: Quito old Town is definitely worth a visit. Lots of narrow, pedestrian only streets. Beautiful church's and great Plazas. We really enjoyed our time here.

title: Quito visit would be incomplete without it
text: This is the place one should visit when in Quito. This place is so alive! It’s like the pulse of Quito! Being the first UNESCO world heritage sites; it would be a shame if you don’t visit this place. Take extra precaution as I read there are many petty thievery in the area. I was with my tour guide and I always felt safe

title: History, architecture, food it’s all here
text: I found this a safe place to be as long as you are aware of your surroundings and not stupid with personal possessions on show etcThere is much to see, experience traditional food and drink. Ecuadorian coffee, chocolate etc. Give it a try you will not be disappointed.

title: Great for a one day stop over
text: Enough to see in the old town for a 24 hour stop over. Clearly old with a history, Quito is safe, clean and surprisingly well organized. Lots of places to eat, drink and sit within the city gives you an insight into living in Central America.

title: Old town beauty.
text: Stay in the historic old town.I have stayed here on 3 separate trips and felt safe every time.  The buildings are well kept and allow for many hours of wandering and wonder.

title: Like dropping into Spain
text: Visiting Old Colonial Quito was like being transported to Spain. The architecture is much different than anything you’ll see outside of Old Town. The Ecuadorian President lives in Old Quito and there are many majestic old churches to see. I’ve been warned that you should watch for pickpockets but I didn’t have any trouble.

This is truly old town feeling- there are so many churches to visit and see - each one is so different and it’s al about the history of Quito here.  It’s fun to people watch and grab something to eat whether it’s food or the pastries.  Them I have nice pictures of the streets and the buildings are so classic.   It’s a must to visit the presidential palace and the churches and on the weekend I believe the have a market where you can buy many Ecuadorian things plus artists that have unique skills and produce amazing crafts.  Love it

title: World Heritage Wonder
text: The Quito Old Town is a must-visit place on your trip to Ecuador. Whether you are stopping over on your way to the Galápagos Islands or headed out to the Amazon Rain Forest, Old Quito is worth a day of exploration. There are old churches, colonial buildings and enough good restaurants and chocolate shops to make this worth your while.Take a cab/Uber to this area. It is generally easy to find a cab and prices are reasonable. While it is a bustling area, note that it is not well-lit in all corners at night. Be aware of your surroundings and you should be fine.For a bit more detail, opt for a walking tour.

title: Super cute
text: Everything is clean and beautiful. Very nice buildings and impressive churches. Take the free walking tour instead of going out by yourself!

title: tour of Quito
text: Quito is an interesting city to visit, with many beautiful churches and Spanish architecture. It was safe, and the food was delicious.

title: Take the free walking tour
text: For the cost of a gratuity you’ll enjoy a three hour walking tour of the historical center of Quito Ecuador

title: A walk in the past
text: If you enjoy history do take some time for Quito. Spectacular churches, all in walking distance. Full during the daytime, with locals and not the tourist crowds that you may find in historic towns and villages in Europe. The tourists are there too, and so are the hawkers, the venders, but the atmosphere is ever so firendly. And the churches are fascinating. (Even if the opening times are unpredictable.) Good for two, even three days. And the hop on hop off tour of Quito is also useful to get a feel of the city.

title: Beautiful history wherever you turn
text: Highly recommend a trip or two to Old Town. Churches, plazas, and small stores line the small and narrow streets. You can feel history at every step. I recommend Basilica San Francisco and Basilica Del Voto Nacional if you want a closer look at churches. Might come back to catch a show at Teatro Bolivar, stunning just from the lobby!

title: pretty
text: tis is a good drive by town, it is very clean and gives you a taste of the the colonial times in quito.

title: Beautiful but watch your pockets
text: WE really enjoyed exploring Quito Old Town - even if one of us lost their phone because of a pick pocket who squeezed tight as we entered a church (of all places).

title: A nice place to explore
text: We really enjoyed exploring the Old Town.  There are several free museums, street theatre and lots else. Plaza San Francisco was a pleasant surprise, especially the labyrinthine Fair Trade shop in the corner.

title: Quaint but too many beggars
text: Historic downtown is great for pictures because of the old architecture, however, the municipality should do a better job about the beggars

title: Rhonda street
text: Old town or (districto  historico) is definitely worth a visit. The grand plaza has some architectural sights as well as restaurants and people. We enjoyed walking in the evening and the presence of police made us feel safe even with younger kids. A visit to Ronda street is a must for the music and bar scene. Live music every ten feet. It can get a little seedy as it gets late so I suggest not staying too late but I did have my older kids who stayed late and enjoyed the vibes. Taxis were plentiful and fair so no problem getting back to your hotel. From the plaza you can walk to the basilica which is also worth seeing if you haven’t toured too many churches. It is still used every day and the towers offer a great view of the city and the sanctuary.

title: Quito Old Town - got to see it.
text: Well worth a look around with plenty of quaint shops and places to eat. Lots of churches and stuff as well.

title: Restaurants Los Geranios
text: Wonderful food! Great service by Irving!It is on the oldest pedestrian road in Quito!Beautiful! If you want good food and good service and a reasonably priced restaurant in Quito’s old town this is for you!

title: Charm of the old city has been lost in the crowds
text: It's full of old buildings, and narrow streets crowded with people. Buskers from other South American nations compete with locals for alms. Where is the charm?  I did not quite feel it!

title: Walk and inspiration
text: Sunday morning is the best time for walking around, the streets are only for people and closed for cars. Enjoy the colourful life of markets and plazas and witness colonial time buildings intact, learn from the historical museums and beautiful lanscapes from the hills. Panecillo, yaku, and Basilica stand out for view points.

title: Discover one of the most impressive colonial centres in Latin America.
text: I walked, enjoyed, took pictures, visited  its gradiose churchers, convents and palaces and I found sometihing new around every corner.Discover the wonders of Quito's history through its streets. Stop for a moment at every corner, enjoy the weather, listen to the locals talking. Enter a popular café or restaurant. Take advantage of the good prices of the small shops.  Visit each wonderful church. Take a nice souvenir of beautiful Quito.

title: Quito, Old Town/Middle of the World and Otavalo
text: The tour began with a visit to the colonial section of Quito, walking along the narrow cobblestone streets taking in the charm of 16th-century Quito. We visited Independence Plaza, the Cathedral, Presidential Palace, and the Archbishop's Palace before visiting La Compañia Church or the Church of San Francisco. Afterwards we visited the Middle of the World Monument, located at a latitude of 0º, while standing with one foot in the Northern and one in the Southern Hemisphere. Quite a treat.  Our tour guide was Alfredo Carrera who is a local that understands the country's history and culture.  He is very experienced and has travelled throughout South America now embarking on his own tour company, Southamerican Road Trippers.  Hoping to take another tour with him soon.

title: Beautiful historic city!
text: We have been travelling all around South America and we can confidently say that Quito has the most preserved historic downtown areas so far. People are lovely, food is great and quite diverse

title: Un holandes en Quito
text: Quito is nice and the old city centre is clean, secure and very nice to visit. The only thing I don't understand is the opening hours for restaurants, many of them closed on regular hours. What is missing are some good places to drink some beers, everything closed and everybody to bed very early. But don't misunderstand me, Quito is nice and a good base to visit the Amazon and the Galapagos.

title: Good town plaza
text: This town square has a great park in the middle. We were lucky to see the welcoming of the president to his palace. The horses, bands and soldiers were a wonderful show.

title: Fun fun!
text: Our hotel was in Old Quito, which had such charm and little boutique cafes, pizzerias and bakeries. Felt like I lived there! Fun to explore!

title: Busy town center
text: We spent a Sunday afternoon here on our own walking tour following a route recommended in the Lonely Planet guidebook. The seemed to be a rather lot of various types of police presence around and we have never seen so many ice cream street vendors anywhere else in our world travels. We saw some neat old architecture and enjoyed climbing the watch tower at the Basilica. Overall we felt a little bit nervous from having read several bad stories about pickpockets but other than seeing many very drunk street people we did fine and there are certainly a lot of families out and about. There were also very many street performers so there seemed to always be something to watch.

title: Safe town
text: I find it odd that people don’t think this area is safe. Don’t let 1 persons bad experience make you think it’s not. I liked this area since it’s pedestrian friendly and allows you to see a bunch of touristy things

title: Thieves are working hard
text: Beautiful old town, beautiful church but please be aware that pick pocketing is happening. A child, probably 12 years old watched my friend zip her iPhone in her pocket. He waited until she was at the end of the pack and unzipped it and tried to get it. Luckily she caught him but we were surprised at how young he was. We also saw another person after theirs had been taken. Wear your backpack on the front.

title: Nice historical town
text: We visited old town Quito with a guide which was very helpful. The town square, buildings and churches were very interesting. There are a number of people that would follow us to buy things but we told them "no" and they left us alone. It is best to explore old town with a guide to ensure safety and obtain their knowledge of the city.

title: Where you really must stay!
text: A classic old town experience and the pedestrian only streets make it feel at times as if you are at a great street fair...yes..it does have its very touristy elements, however this IS Quito...and while the traffic in Quito is challenging at times, staying in Old Town insures you get the true feel of the city as it was...then take a taxi to the newer sections and be glad to return to the quaint charm of your hotel at night!

title: Great welcome to Quito
text: Best part of the city. 2 days there. Sundays are the best with lots of street entertainment! TeleferiQo is also a good place for the day but aim to go early as afternoons are often cloudy.

title: Wantara Suites
text: My wife and I traveled to Ecuador and stayed at three different places over a month.  Staying at Wantara in Old Town Quito was by far the best.  Best security and access, best view, best porch and gardens, best bed, best kitchen and best shower by far.  It is highly recommended

title: Nice Old Town
text: Lovely old town to walk around in.  Plenty of police presence, just enough to make it feel safe without feeling like military occupation.  Some of the restaurants and shops very upscale, but many just small shops not well maintained.  Plenty of shopping and dining. We had a fabulous lunch at La Purisima.  Nice drink and view at the rooftop bar Vista Hermosa near Plaza Grande, but the other Vista Hermosa (Itchimbia) is supposed to be nice just outside Old Town (same owners).

title: Amazing architecture
text: Quito was picturesque nestled by Andes on all sides.  The people there were overwhelmingly friendly and helpful.  We got up early to see the city before the day got started and I would highly recommend it.  A certain level of caution must be taken with cameras and purses, but it was a great part of the city.

title: Spent a day before Galapagos
text: A day is more than enough time here. There are some nice churches and the gondola up the mountain was fun. A few good shops.

title: Quito Colonial.... the city dreamed for Spain
text: It is an historial, traditional, architectonic. treasure. Beautiful buildings, interesting museum and the people.... the people is the best!

title: Magical place!
text: Quito's Old Town is a UNESCO World Heritage site.   When you walk the streets you are walking colonial Ecuador.   The churches, the houses, the shops, the food, everything takes you back to the time in history.

title: Great evening stroll !
text: The romance of walking streets in Quito'sold town is magnificent....close your eyes and you can easily imagine what was happening here hundreds of years ago.Open your eyes and see the amazing architecture of the old city and residences built into the side of the hill. breathtaking for sure.

title: A world heritage area
text: It is filled with beautiful churches in baroque, gothic and neoclassical styles. There are also, convents, palaces, and several pretty public squares.

title: A Great Place to Explore!
text: We were a large group of mixed ages (25 in total) and we really enjoyed exploring Quito Old Town. Everyone was incredibly friendly and even though most of us did not speak Spanish, it was easy to get around.

title: Old City Charm - Worth Seeing
text: We had a conducted tour of the old Town, partly by bus and partly walking. There's plenty to see and lots of history to discover. Why not visit a chocolate factory and try the samples?

title: Old town
text: What is considered old town is a nice but small area. It is ok but nothing different to other city capitals in Latin America. Some few churches, one not old at all, compress this zone. It looks clean and colorful

title: My favorite part of living here
text: Although I lived farther north, old town was my favorite part of the city. I would often hop on the bus and head down there just to walk around for a few hours and people watch. My favorite areas were around La Plaza Grande and La Iglesia San Francisco.

title: Beautiful city
text: This unesco world site is worth a visit inspire of the steep hills and high altitude.  It's worthwhile to walk up and down all of the streets to see all of the amazing building.

title: Colonial old town at its best
text: I've been to my fair share of colonial cities and I think Quito is absolutely beautiful and well preserved. You will walk through many colonial style churches, streets, buildings, and parks that you are almost transported to this time. You have to visit the Plaza Grande main square, Iglesia de San Francisco, Basílica del Voto Nacional, Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesus, among others.

title: Small but nice
text: It is very small and not too giant churches to see.  But is a early part of South America history.   The city is in the middle of green mountains, beautiful!

title: Enhance your travels with the rich history of Quito
text: We were more than pleasantly surprised by the beautifully preserved architecture and culture of Quito Old Town. So much history and so much beauty. I felt like I was in Europe at times but you get both the Inca history and the Spanish Colonial. Anyone would truly enhance their travel experience by allowing a few days to appreciate the rich history of this place.

title: So much to see
text: Wandering the old town is best appreciated with a guide. They are able to give you a sense of the history and the relationship of the buildings and squares. It is fairly hilly, so good walking shoes are a must. Much of the area is blocked off from traffic and there are many people on the streets. Vendors are plentiful, but they are polite and don’t harass you. Several types of police patrol constantly, making the area feel safe. There are many small shops interspersed among the churches and squares, and it appears that man locals come into the Old Town to shop.

title: Stunning Spanish and Moorish architecture
text: The main square, Plaza Grande, in Quito’s Old Town is surrounded by buildings of power representing God (Catedral Metroplitana; free tours), the Catholic Church (The Bishop’s Palace; now a colonnaded row of small shops you can tour on your own), and the Government of Ecuador (Palacio de Gobierno; free tours).Plaza Grande is just the beginning of the delights of Quito’s Old Town. There are many other sights to explore: museums, churches, restaurants, additional plazas and shops.We were in a small tour group and the 2 1/2 hours we spent in Old Town barely scratched the surface of the history behind these magnificent buildings and their architecture. We only had the opportunity to enter two of the seven historical Catholic churches in Old Town. However, the two we visited were awesome. (FYI: no photographs are allowed inside any of the churches😢)FYI: You could easily spend two full days just in Old Town touring museums and churchesKey:Photo 1:  Church and convent of St Francis Photo 2: Iglesia de Santo DomingoPhoto 3: Metropolitan Carhedral of QuitoPhoto 4: Compañia de Jesus

title: Great place to visit
text: We took a cab from our hotel in the business district to Old Town.  It took about 20 minutes, but we are so glad we went.  We spent much of the day just wandering around looking at the squares, the shops, the people and the churches.  We had a delightful time and would return to Ecuador based on our experience here.

title: Special place
text: Great day walking through the old town with our guide. Numerous places to see including churches, other buildings of interest and just to sample the vibrancy of the place. Didn't feel threatened in fact the locals were very friendly.

title: HIghly recommend
text: Quito, the capital of Ecuador, was founded in the 16th century on the ruins of an Inca city and stands at an altitude of 2,850 m. Despite the 1917 earthquake, the city has the best-preserved, least altered historic center in Latin America. The monasteries of San Francisco and Santo Domingo, and the Church and Jesuit College of La Compañía, with their rich interiors, are pure examples of the 'Baroque school of Quito', which is a fusion of Spanish, Italian, Moorish, Flemish and indigenous art.

title: UNESCO World Heritage Site
text: Yo!  No need to ‘plain.  Go see it. Take lots of pics,  go into every place, eat and drink, watch the locals and lookout for the tourists

title: Hotel Mama Cuchura
text: Fabulous boutique hotel with friendly helpful staff. The rooms were beautifully presented with a rooftop restaurant/ bar affording panoramic views over the city of Quito. Welcome drinks on arrival and a mini dessert in your room each evening after dinner.

title: BEWARE!!
text: The last day of my trip and a pick pocket stole my wallet while sightseeing in Old Town. This is very serious with identity theft etc...huge hassle cancelling all my bank cards. Wish I'd never stepped foot here.

title: Beautiful
text: Steeped in history. Beautiful. Felt safe. A lot of cafes. Near to museums and many churches. Good transport links.

title: Enjoyable on a spring Sunday
text: We walked Old Town as rainy season turned into a beautiful spring day.  Everyone was out...families, street vendors, musicians and artists.  A historical area made more enjoyable by strolling with the people of Ecuador an a sunny day.

title: Plaza grand
text: An array of coffee shops and places to shop or to eat.  I recommend indulging in the hot chocolate; it's incomparable.  In the evening, go to La Ronda for a great meal and music everywhere.

title: Beautiful plaza and area
text: This is the first UNESO sight! Lovely plaza with many places to grab a drink or meal. Lots of people trying to sell goods on the street, which is not legal in Quito. Must see if visiting- streets are blocked off, no cars!

title: Best Historical Downtown in Latin America
text: Quito is by far the best historical downtown in Sourh America. To begin with is a World Historical Site. It has many beautiful churches among which the best in every sense is La Compañia. The only comparison is the church is the Church of the Spullee Blood in St Petersburg, Russia. Besides he temples, there are monasteries dates back to 1540, very well preserved and with amazing museums inside. Worth the visit 100%

title: Spanish colonialism in architecture
text: Fascinating range of buildings.  Good range of shops, museums and squares.  Bulging with tourist police so felt perfectly safe.

title: A Must when in Quito
text: A charming area where locals gather especially on Sunday.  Many great churches and buildings.  Wandering an 8-10 block area around the plaza is a great history lesson.  People are so nice and the area clean and safe.  Don't miss the upper plaza of San Francisco.

title: Totally overrated - unless you are into ''churches''
text: We were highly disappointed with Quito and would not have visited it had we known how little there is too see. Plus the city appears very poor, houses are not maintained, there are no nice cafes or shops. Plus you have to be careful as the city is not safe for unwary travelers.Lots of churches though, full of golden decor - painful to see in such a poor country.

title: Interesting and busy old part of Quito
text: We had a guide to take us round and point out the interesting sites.  We can highly recommend the cathedrals in the street lf the 7 crosses, and the church and coffee shop in the main plaza.

title: Nice to walk around the city centre
text: We went here with our own tour guide, which was nice as we could easily move everywhere and adapt the program. The city centre is very nice, especially the square where the president resides.

title: Picturesque, but...
text: I've been to many "old towns" and Quito's is pleasant enough, but not truly memorable. It's also quite hilly, so if you're unaccustomed to the altitude, pedestrian exploring can be tiring.

title: Worth a wander
text: We’ve seen quainter, more colourful old towns in this part of the world but Quito’s is worth a few hours’ walk. A number good buskers - and look for some weirder things  on offer from street sellers. One guy would even clip your nails! Plenty of eateries. Follow the routes suggested by tourist maps - it worked for us.

title: Authentic
text: I enjoyed a brief stay in the Historic Center of Quito and found it very authentic since regular people continue to live and do business as usual in this historic area unlike places like Cartagena or even Old San Juan where the historic centers have become clearly touristic. You can be really busy just visiting the Churches including the Basilica. Be aware that on Mondays many places close.

title: The feel of Ecuador
text: You can stay in the business district of just about any city in the world but those with old town areas are special. Experience the old architecture and walk the streets full of history.

title: Must See in Ecuador
text: We spent three week in Quito for business and pleasure and pleasurable it was. We stayed in Old Town for a week and loved it. The architecture is amazing. The centro of Quito is walkable and safe. Great restaurants, museums, churches and historic areas can occupy many days. Some tourists prefer other neighborhoods in Quito, but if you want to feel the pulse of Quito, Old Town is the place to be.

title: Beautiful Architecture, Amazing Churches, and Scarves for $2.50 - All the Good Stuff!
text: We spent 2 days in Old Town Quito last week, on our way to the Galapagos. For photographers, the buildings are spectacular and the people are gorgeous. Like any popular "old town," there are lots of street vendors and entertainers begging for your attention and $. We were lucky enough to visit with Latin Adventures and our guide Diego helped us hit the highlights quickly, and gave us great historical insights. The churches there are magnificent, especially the Notre Dame-inspired Basilica. If you have time, climb the stairs as far up as you can go and you'll be rewarded with a beautiful view of the city.The street vendors are mostly indigenous women who are dressed in traditional garb (including wool on hot days!), often with a baby strapped to their backs. They have stacks of scarves that make great souvenirs - we got them for 2/$5, and you can negotiate. We also found a gorgeous alpaca scarf for $10. They, and our guide, insisted that they were made in Ecuador (not China). They're really quite beautiful.We had lunch at a cool place called Cafe Del Fraile, which had a gorgeous indoor courtyard. The shrimp was great, and it was so nice to find a Diet Coke (keep in mind that you always have to ask for a glass with ice).On our last day in town we visited Old Town with another tour company and spent WAY too much time at a local chocolate factory. But we managed to visit the La Virgen del Panecillo - the magnificent statue of the Virgin Mary overlooking the city.

title: a quick run through
text: We had a walk through the center of the old town. We passed many small shops crowded on the side streets, selling to tourists. We had a very long visit to a very ornately decorated Spanish cathedral. The best part of this Quito tour was visiting a museum of pre-Columbian artifacts. That was very interesting and enjoyed that.

title: Top Unesco Heritage place
text: Quito old town is a place that need to be highly considerer on your visit to Ecuador. Apart from its enchanting architecture and history, it also is a place to meet local people and understand their culture and customs.

title: Lovely place to spend a few days
text: We stayed in Old Town for four days, and enjoyed wandering around the narrow streets and plazas.  We did start with the free walking tour from Community Hostel which helped us get our bearings.  After that, we still encountered something unexpected every day, from all the gold leaf and indigenous influences in many of the churches to interesting shops along pedestrian stairways to the narrow, steep stairs leading to the observation decks in the La Basilica de Voto Nacional tower.  There was much more we would have liked to see than we could fit into our limited time there.

title: Interesting but crowded
text: We enjoyed the old town but it was quite crowded. The people were very friendly.  There are women selling scarves for a very low price. I believe they are 2 for $5. Maybe a little more but they are well worth the price. Great gifts.

title: We felt safe
text: Quito is a large city and there is so much to see. Old Town is a must stop.  We felt safe, there is plenty of food, and the sights will appeal to everyone.  Be certain to visit the square and people watch.

title: First visit to Quito
text: We spent 3 days in the old town Quito area on our way home from Cuenca. We were amazed at how large the old city is and how dense the population is. We walked around a lot and also did the hop on hop off bus which I am doing a review of separately. The architecture is very interesting and the people we met were very friendly and helpful. One night was very nice with no rain so residents were out and about shopping, visiting friends and partaking of the foid being prepared by the street vendors. We met a lovely older lady cooking chicken on a bbq grill. She had really drawn a crowd and everyone was enjoying eating so we gave it a try. It was very good and relaxed sitting on the curb while we ate. We went to a bakery and had some great sweets for dessert and took some stuff back to our room to have for breakfast the next morning. We also went to a couple of restaurants more in the tourist area the other two nights but found restaurants pretty expensive.

title: Holidays and Rainforests
text: Quito is a very interesting and somewhat hilly place! You need strong legs and lungs for this city! As a stop off place on the way to the Amazon rainforest it is ideally placed.Lots of interesting museums and art galleries not least the chocolate museum which give you free tastings yummy 😋

title: The best in the world!
text: Dozens of churches covered in gold leaf, art from the time of the Spanish colony, showing integration with native cultures.

title: Interesting Old Town
text: My friend and I visited the Old Town area during a recent trip to Ecuador. The day we were there most of the square was blocked off with numerous police. This has locals as well as other tourists disappointed. We were able to walk around the perimeter and view the surrounding historic buildings.There are numerous beautiful buildings and churches in the Old Town. Also, many restaurants. We also visited a pedestrian street with shops and cafes. It is not as beautiful or interesting as other central or South American UNESCO World Heritage cities but definitely worth spending a few hours exploring.

title: Nice old town
text: The old historical town is worth a visit if you have a day to spare in Quito. We walked up all the way to the Virgen lookout and it gave us a nice idea and view of the city. The main square in town was closed for local people unless accompanied by tourists what seemed a bit odd to us.

title: Walk, walk, walk
text: Enjoyed the whole area with a free walking tour, the varied architecture and the history of the area.

title: Old Town
text: Had a blast walking they old town with out personal guide     Learned a lot about the culture and some things that I didn’t realize (Panama Hats - Made in Ecuador!!!)

title: Beautiful
text: The old town is a jewel cloaked in its own time! Hundreds of years away from modern Quito. Fantastic during Holy Week!

title: Vacation
text: Did a guided city tour in the late aft If you decide to explore on tour own make sure you have a map and plenty of time the old town is laid out in a Spanish colonial styleThe main square is worth a visit There are 7 churches to visit but they close at 5pm so set aside time to exploreThe metro is under construction

title: Great Old Town
text: I really enjoyed exploring Quito's Old Town. There are many churches, some of which require a fee to enter. I was impressed at the number of police officers observing the area and never felt unsafe. I was there during the weekend day, so it was quite crowded. The streets can be narrow and the area has some hills, but I found this quarter to be very charming and would definitely visit again.

title: A busy place...
text: It’s a good place to walk around and possibly have something to eat. Frequented by locals and tourists alike, it does get crowded at times. There are also a few more touts than we’ve encountered in other South American cities. Because it has the Presidential Palace near the centre, there is a lot of security.

title: Loved it!
text: We took a free walking tour by Communityhostel in Quito. What a great day!, We got to watch the changing of the Guards at the Presidential Palace. (first Monday every month). Happy walking here!

title: Nice place to wander around
text: If you go to Quito you really need to go to the Old Town.  Be sure and check out he marketplace for some cheap, local food.  Also, the La Ronda street is wonderful to amble down.

title: The "OLD Town" of any city is fascinating!  (at least to me)
text: Be prepared to do a lot of walking up and down hilly streets.  And buy an ice-cream ($0.50 ) or two from theubiquitous street vendors. If walking is not your shtick, then take a trolley bus !  - Only $0.25 (or maybe 50 cents -I forget.And remember!  The currency of Ecuador is the US dollar!  No exchange is necessary (if you are American)

title: Caught up in a protest but never mind
text: Certainly worth wandering around and taking in the architecture and people watching. Of course plenty of churches to see and the street vendors are not too aggressive. Plus the band was playing outside the presidential palace.

title: Amazing good and bad
text: Parts of the old town of Quito are UNESCO World Heritage - and indeed a lot of the houses and churches are really amazing. But - the pollution of the exhaust emissions especially from the blue busses are amazing dangerous. That borders on assault.

title: A must visit
text: The old town in Quito is a must-see. Beautiful colonial buildings some with courtyards and many accessible to the general public free-of-charge. Just walk around and take in the history. Public squares, churches, statues, and since we were there before upcoming elections, some political speeches and crowds. Nothing too overwhelming. Of course, a guide really helps! Street vendors are ubiquitous but are not pushy. Prices are very reasonable.

title: Too commercialized
text: The old town looks great and it is clean and safe. I think, in some areas, the amount of signage devalues the original buildings architectural detailing.

title: A Must
text: If you're going to Quito then going to Old Town should be on your list. Old time churches and cathedrals, market places, restaurants, and friendly people.Most of the area is traffic free too.

title: Great place for a morning stroll or spend the day
text: Lots of museums and churches to visit. If you not interested in those things, have a coffee in one of the plazas and people watch.

title: Plan to spend the day
text: We were happy that we had participated in a city walking tour. Took a couple of hours but gave us all the highlights. It was up to you to return to any stops that you wanted to explore further! Lots of interesting shops and restaurants... interesting churches and cathedrals... lots of fun street vendors!

title: Lovely old town, cobblestone streets, great architecture
text: A great way to spend a morning/afternoon is to wander around the old town of Quito. It can be a little touristy but the cobblestone streets and architecture are worth a look. Many of the old churches and monuments are located here so you can tick quite a few boxes off here.

title: A UNESCO World Heritage site
text: Old Town is a must-see in Quito, and a nice contrast to its more modern areas.  Filled with old churches, restaurants, quaint shops and cobblestone streets, as well as the good people of Quito simply conducting their everyday business.  It was filled with people the day we visited.

title: Old town
text: Full of people during the day and a wonderful place for people watching, especially on Sunday if the weather is good.

title: INTERESTING
text: An interesting area with lots of history.  Level walk to all the churches – couple very over-the-top.  Many open areas.

title: Beautiful city
text: My daughters and I did our own walking tour of old town, such a beautiful city with so much history.  We were there on a Sunday and there were local merchants selling goods, street performers and food carts all around Independence Square, it was a great way to get the local flavor.The churches are amazing (some had a small fee) in the area and you must see the La Ronda neighborhood, it’s beautiful and well preserved.

title: Great introduction to Quito and Ecuador history and culture
text: We walked around the Quito old town in the morning after spending the evening in the La Ronda district. The historic district is surprisingly large, including the cathedral, the many squares and municipal building. The zoning restrictions for business signs are a nice touch, but could be enforced a bit better. Everywhere we walked felt safe, and the are is policed by a dedicated Policía Turística force.

title: Quito old town worth some time
text: Quito is an interesting city, we enjoyed the old town very much and went there several times.  There are interesting restaurants, and much historical to take in.

title: Charming place but...
text: Go early since it can be very touristy by noon.  We we lucky in that we like to run in the morning and can see the main plaza with its beautiful churches and buildings with balconies before the rest of the population wakes up. Most places charge a steep price for entrance for the non-natives, which is a shame.

title: Nice place to walk
text: The walk around old town is amazing with all the churches and interesting stores. It is quaint and absolutely worth it.

title: Great Historical City but NOT Safe at Night
text: We enjoyed spending time in Old Town Quito and learning Quito’s unique history.  The city had a lot of culture and numerous beautiful churches.  We enjoyed visiting Ecuadorian chocolate shops, the beautiful gilded churches, and learning about the history of the city.  There is a tremendous amount of poverty in the city and I did not feel safe walking to dinner in the evening.   The much needed food lines were located in San Fransisco square and other spots that are considered Quito’s most visited spots for tourists.

title: Great unesco site
text: The old city is wonderful place to visit and see. The area is well kept and wonderful condition.  The cobblestone streets and building were great to see

title: Quito Old Town
text: This is the place to stay, eat, drink and be merry. This is the place for me in Quito! It's very safe and there always appears to be a lot happening.

title: A gem.
text: The Old Town is lovely to explore, whether during the day or in the evening. There are so many beautifully preserved buildings from the colonial era. Start in the Plaza Grande, use a tourist map and explore all the lovely streets and squares, churches, museums and restaurants. Take it slowly until you have acclimatised to the altitude.

title: Charming - lots of construction
text: Meandering around the streets of Quito's Old Town was great fun - wandering in and out of churches, shops and chocolatiers.  The atmosphere in their many plazas/squares was lively and great for people-watching.  But when we were there there was a lot of construction and renovation going on.  You had to be careful to dodge construction sites and try to keep tarps, scaffolding, etc. out of your pictures.  I'm very glad they are investing in the renovations, though.  The city is beautiful and should be preserved.

title: I have fallen in love with Quito
text: If you get the chance to stay in  Downtown Quito, do it! It is the UNESCO centre of Quito city and is full of activity and charm. If you stay there, everything is within walking distance from museums, to churches, the Presidential Square and Palace, to artesan shops and eateries. Look through doorways into the inner squares beyond to find shops, restaurants and more tucked away inside. I have spent the last 5 months travelling in South America and I have fallen in love with Downtown Quito. A must see on your travel list.

title: For Spanish Colonial Towns, Spectacular
text: From beautiful churches to a vibrant street scene, I think Quito may just be in the top 10 worldwide of superb old town centers.Unfortunately we only had one day in old town but made the best of it with two walking tours from Strawberry Tours (see separate review).  One time, alone, we needed directions and two locals (presumably) managed to summit the language mountain with smiles and we made our destination.The only precautionary note is to be aware that this Old Town is built on hills and you will be going up or down well over half of your time.

title: Colonial Latin America at its best
text: Its interesting to walk the many streets past myriads of colonial buildings and churches.  There is a lot to see, and for the most part, it's free.

title: Intresting
text: We then went on to explore La Ronda, which is a historic narrow lane.   This lane has a strong French influence, as it turns out the locals wanted to establish a different atmosphere then the hated Spanish had left in the country for so many years.

title: Charming colonial architecture and ice cream on a walking tour
text: We followed a walking tour from the Lonely Planet which started at San Fransisco Plaza and ended at a beautiful park. There were plenty of street entertainers and street food vendors, beautiful churches to explore and yummy ice cream to enjoy on a bench in one of the many green spaces. Some vendors will even deliver ice cream through car windows in the street. It is a lovely way to spend a day, wandering the narrow cobblestone streets and enjoying the culture.

title: Twilight magic!
text: Take the time to go here around 4pm. The shops will still be open, you can have an afternoon coffee and see the city transform into golden lights on the many ornate, historical buildings and the Winged Mary! There is a lot of police presence. Around 7pm, we were good to go back to your hotel.

title: Quito in the mountains
text: First off, Quito is old and growing to be modern.  You will find street vendors almost everywhere you go. There is much to see in Quito Old Town so I suggest finding an official guide or tour. There are many non-official and I would not recommend hiring them. Official guides have college training and you'll find the England better and the stories and history more accurate. The new Quito airport is a good 45 minutes outside of town. Make arrangements for transportation ahead if possible or plan on a rapid $25-30 taxi ride.Do go to the equator!  It's 60 minutes out of town and worth the history as well as the Equator stamp in your passport.

title: Always interesting
text: Working in Quito I was in the old town many times. Always new places to explore. Great spot to eat,people watch and be surprised by new sights

title: Totally Different!
text: This was a first visit for me to South America and I was struck by it's totally different character as compared to the UK. 10000 feet above sea level and surrounded by the Andes it's an imposing environment that is at the same time spectacular. The hubbub which is the old city centre is vibrant, fascinating and colourful. Echoes of its colonial past are present in the architecture exemplified by it's gothic cathedral. Occasional sightings of hummingbirds emphasises its tropical feel. Well worth a visit

title: Unsafe
text: Nice to see but unsafe! Watch out for your belongings. Me and my friends were walking around in the touristy streets at 7 pm and some guy with a machete grabbed my bag and ran away. Felt a weird vibe in the city throughout our whole stay.

title: Unique experience.
text: We visited old town on a Sunday.  The town square was full of families enjoying the sunshine. Street vendors selling every kind of food imaginable. It was super cool.

title: Very interesting
text: Interesting guided tour through the old town - we were on hop on hop off. Amazing guide Erika had lots of wonderful information. Highly recommend!

title: Go Galapagos Day Tour
text: Mirium and Luis gave us a wonderful and informative tour of the City and it's offerings.  Also recommended dinner at URKO and Ecuadorian food restaurant which was truly an amazing experience.

title: Interesting Old Town
text: Very busy Old Town, but definitely worth a visit, to see some of the many Churches, in particular “Cathedral Maria De Jesus Quito” also known as the Gold church everything inside is made from Gold unfortunately no photographs can be taken, entrance fee of $5, Another nice place to see is the Rhonda very famous street and the many wee individual shops with handmade goods along the way.

title: History and Culture
text: Sauntered around Old Town for a couple of days; once with a free guided tour through the Community Hostel (10:30 start time) and once on our own.  The first was good for getting a historical sense of the place.  The second was good for a perspective of the people and pace of this culture.  While there are some warning about venturing out in Old Town, there certainly isn't any problem during the day and at night (at least until 9 pm) while people are on the streets.

title: Very interesting town
text: A lot of the historic centre is being renovated and it all looks very good.  Well worth a couple of days here.

title: Fascinating
text: It was wonderful to wander the streets.Magnificent buildings . Opulent churches.Lovely seeing people about their everyday lifes

title: Beautiful
text: Really beautiful old town with lots of buildings now cool South American bars and restaurants. Expect deafening music as they try and out do one another across the street. Good vibe and fab places to sample local food.

title: Book a walking tour
text: We booked a walking tour to see all the sites in Old Town and learn the history/culture and political situation of Ecuador.  It was extremely interesting.  The tour guide was great at recommending restaurants in the area and neighbourhoods to avoid.

title: Nice place to walk around
text: There are many little shops and restaurants tucked into small openings in buildings. Very informal and the markets are busy.

title: Wonderful area to walk, prices too high
text: This is a beautiful area, most of the churches are amazing to see inside and out. We love walking cities when we travel so that part was enjoyable.But a lot of the shops assume tourists have unlimited pockets, and have no grasp of the market value of their products. Most prices were higher than if we bought the same item in the US. We are not a fan of paying too much for something that costs more than at home, and then lug that item home in weighed suitcases.But again it's nice to walk around and view the history.

title: Delightful, Memorable Quito!
text: So much to see from the architecture, plazas, churches and small shops. We booked a private, 1/2 day tour with a local that was fantastic. We learned so much about Quito and its history while we walked through Old Town, touring several of the churches, stopping to meet artisans, shopkeepers, hat makers and chocolate confectioners. We visited San Francisco Market, where our guide bought several fruits produced in Ecuador which were delicious! From the cobbled streets with typical balconies overflowing with geraniums, we also visited the Manos en La Ronda which has artisans in their workshops. We also visited El Panecillo which provides a panoramic view and features the 150-foot high monument  of the Virgen de Quito.

title: It's a "gotta see!"
text: It's a friendly area. The park is a lively area with Ecuadorian charm. Wish there were quality restaurants in the area.

title: Beautiful Old Town
text: We spent most of our time in Quito here. The streets and plazas are pedestrian friendly with many lovely historical buildings and churches. Many entertaining street performers in area and plenty of places to get a meal or snack. We felt quite safe here, although we did have a problem of sorts elsewhere in the city.

title: Nice place to stroll
text: Good place to stroll, people watch and get a bite to eat.  Lovely old city.  There are lots of museums and churches to pop into.

title: Very fascinating old town
text: Heading down from the Basilica on Venezuela toward the Independence Square we enjoyed the different style buildings. There is an active security presence on the main streets. The people in their varied shops are pleasant and respond to greetings. No pressure for the most part, walking vendors of fruit or scarfs are slightly more insistent. The number of gorgeous churches was a bit of a surprise. I can imagine a Sunday with the population coming out and going to their various congregations.

title: Where It's At
text: If you don't don't do anything else go here.  Churches, shopping, museums, it's the place to be.  Start here first.  Plenty to do for a half-day.

title: Quito Old Town
text: Best visited in the evening when,I believe, it becomes a hive of activity. During the morning it was exceptionally quiet with only a chocolate shop open and the Spinning Top shop.

title: Enjoyed our Old Town Experience
text: It was great fun to walk around old town and visit a few sites and go into a few stores. Everything was very safe and the streets were  very clean.  Highly recommend spending a few hours or more in this area if you are in Quito.

title: Assaulted on my way to Old Town
text: We didn’t make it to Old Town today because on our way there I was attacked. We are both very seasoned travelers and have been to many developing countries, but for some reason this city felt different. Today we were walking on a side street at around 3pm toward the old town area. The exact area is the corner of Vargas and Manabi. As we turned the corner my fiancé was walking a few feet in front of me. A man ran up behind me with a crowd of people around and put me in a choke hold while securing my arm. I couldn’t breathe and was struggling as the noise made it hard for my partner to hear. The man was reaching into my front pocket trying to pull out a small camera (my mistake for putting it there). I finally got loose enough to scream and as my fiancé turned the man pushed me and took off running. Luckily he did not harm me further or take anything. I was shaking and hysterical. I always heard Quito was safe and now I’m terrified to walk around. Be careful and avoid this area!

title: Great Place to Stay
text: Loved walking around old town. Can do it on foot in a day or two. Loved the local artists and shops, and chocolate! Really enjoyable place, and the hotel La Casona de la Ronda was spectacular!

title: Well worth a visit, but can get crowded.
text: Any old town is better than a modern one.  So is Quito.Getting there is a bit of a struggle battling the traffic.  But most of the old town is pedestrian only.  Some nice squares and statues, but no English descriptions.  Lovely narrow streets and painted houses.Can get crowded like any place so take care.  Lots of people selling everything but they were respectful of a 'no thanks'

title: Wandering Quito's older, somewhat somnambulant quarters...
text: A 30 minute bus ride from the tourist-popular Plaza Foch area gets you to the Old Town. The streets are often on a steep incline, but level out to traverse the barrios and approach the central plaza. A few block in any direction reveals the real Quito life which for many is arduous at best and yet they are ready to acknowledge greetings of 'hola' y 'buenos dias'.Plenty of local food vendors of varying quality and local cafes and bars, hotels and hostels.I visited in August 2017 to witness the night time Light Festival and it was well worth spending hours wandering, resting, snacking and mingling with a slow-moving tourist and local populace.Less frenetic and less traffic than central Quito it is worth knowing: "Ecuador’s capital city is home to one of Latin America’s best-preserved Colonial Districts, which happened to one of the very first places on the planet to receive UNESCO’s title as a World Heritage Site and, not to mention – the first city in South America to announce its independence from Spain. In light of the latter, Quito’s Fiesta de la Luz serves as a commemorative event that pays homage to the country’s history."

title: Very interesting.
text: Very nice Colonial architecture.  Take good walking shoes for the cobblestones.  Quite a number of interesting restaurants and courtyards for lunch or dinner.

title: Have a free tour of old town from community hostel!
text: Many places give a free tour  like community hostel and then please tip the tour guide because they share a wealth of information with you!

title: Atmospheric old town
text: Quito's Old Town is atmospheric with beautiful old buildings particularly churches, a pretty main square with the Presidential Palace down one side.  It was safe and comfortable to walk round.

title: A lovely old historic town
text: My father and I recently visited on our way to the Galápagos. A very charming and historic part of town. A very walkable city that has Spanish architecture along with charming parks and shops. I would recommend to anyone.

title: A tour into beautiful old Quito
text: Historic Quito centre is a national humanity patrimony and many views, fine old architecture, delicious local food, and cosy public places are the perfect environment for a safe and enjoyable walking circuit. A time for a long lasting remembrance

title: Very walkable
text: The churches and open plazas are wonderful.  Many of the roads were closed to cars so it is very easy to walk around.  For safety I'd recommend being there during daylight hours.

title: Beautiful architecture
text: My friend and I spent the whole day walking all around old town exploring the beautiful architecture, plazas and sampling a lot of chocolate!  We had read a couple of negative reviews regarding safety. However, as long as you use common sense, there is nothing to be afraid of. Walk with a purpose and don’t look like fearful tourist that has no confidence! In addition there was an incredible police presence everywhere we went in the city. We felt safe.

title: Amazing Quito!
text: Quito Old Town (Centro Histórico) is an architectural masterpiece and the colonial heart of Quito. There are dozens of grandiose churches, convents and palaces, many looming over pretty public squares.

title: Up & Down or across each street
text: The old town is a must if you are in Quito.  Remember that Quito is built on a hill so take a look and try to go across rather than up and down each street especially with the high altitude.  There is plenty to see, Plaza Grande with the museum and Cathedral,  old buildings, La Ronda, and coffee shops a plenty.

title: afternoon visit
text: There's a lot to see so wear decent comfortable footwear. remember also that the city is at altitude so don't over do it. Get a map with a couple of suggested routes on it and use the main square as a reference point to explore on foot. be alert to the potential pick pockets who are known to use distraction techniques but don't let that spoil your enjoyment of the culture.

title: Interesting
text: We enjoyed just walking about on our own, stopping into little shops, people watching  and admiring the architecture.

If you are in Quito this is the first place to explore. Go to the main plaza and have a coffee, ice cream or snack and do alittle people watching.  Then proceed to the churches and excellent museums and art exhibits,

title: One of the best Old Town centres in South America
text: I have travelled all over South America and I must say, Quito's Old Town is the best I have seen.Firstly, it isn't actually that touristy. What I mean by that is that it is a "working town" so you see many more typical shops than tourist shops.The main square had its share of tourists however, there are a lot of locals as well. There are many retirees who dress just like they were going to work in a bank (suit, tie etc) and they come down to the square to hang out. So nice to see.There are many, many beautiful churches you can visit. Every one of them are unique and interesting.The "old town" is so large that it would take you more than a week to explore all of the streets.Without a doubt, one of my favourite in South America.

title: Awesome
text: We were able to walk from our airbnb into the historic district. Great restaurants, architecture and food shops.

title: Interesting and well worth a visit
text: An old city with lots of history and interesting architecture. We are not interested much in cathedrals, churches and the like, but there are lots worth visiting for those so inclined. Ditto for museums and public buildings, but again there are lots worth going to see Lots of places to walk and cabs or uber are cheap.Very glad we chose to come for a few days.   They are days well spent.

title: Should not be missed but not a favorite
text: We visited here recently when passing through Quito. The buildings were interesting but the police presence was really striking such that one wondered if it was really safe. Although this is a major attraction here we did not see very many other tourists.

title: Beautiful in places
text: The Old Town in Quito is wonderfully beautiful and historical. It was, for a reason, the world's first UNESCO World Heritage Site. It is seemingly untouched by modernisation and therefore is a great place to observe south american history in its purest format. Despite this it is a bit dangerous at night so I would advice you not to go out when it is dark. There is much to see and do in the day however such as monuments, plazas, calle ronda and some of the many cathedrals.

title: Old Quito Town
text: We used the Free Tour system which is provided by an expert and covers around 3 hours of gentle walking around the notable sites. Our guide was Fernando and he provided an excellent tour giving us both the past and recent history of Equador.Although classified as a free tour everyone in the group puts a few bucks in for the guides time. In our case it ranged from 10-20 USD per person, which was well worth it 👍

title: Quito city
text: For people who want to know a paradise within ecuador, I recommend the city of Quito, in my opinion I give you to know a little about it, it is a very beautiful city starting with the gastronomy that is very spectacular, one of its special dishes we have this sweet called "pristiños" this consists of a bread dough figure covered by a macbifica sweet layer of panela, another macnifica quality are its majestic churches, we can find the historical center, the church of the basilica, and chairs where they count a sinmumer of stories, myths and legends for tourists who want to know a little more about the history of Quito, currently cataloged by tourist organizations as quito luz de america

title: difficult to walk at street level
text: was okay but not that exciting.  diesel exhaust from busses spewing fumes from below the bus made it difficult to walk around

title: Definitely worth a day!
text: Great place to walk around  Seems like a church or museum on every corner in some stretches. Great photo ops where the streets frame buildings that are on hills.

title: Living history
text: Classic buildings with a busy city feel. Go to the Tourist info centre in the Grand Plaza first to get your bearings and ask about the different sites to visit. Meanwhile enjoy a thick hot chocolate in the cafe whilst choosing your itinerary. Most attractions are within easy walking distance.

title: Pretty, but unless you're into churches there's not much to see...
text: We spent a happy afternoon walking around Quito and it's pretty with interesting architecture. If you're into churches, there are 27 to see.We organised tours for the rest of our time in Quito as there wasn't much more to see.

title: Stunning!
text: I visited Quito's old town 2 weeks ago with my kids and parents. We were amaze watching the architecture of the churchs and buildings. Everything was walk distance so we were able to see a lot in a couple pf hours. The people around was friendly and you have nice restaurants around if you feel hungry. Definitely a place to  visit.

title: Take the free Walkiing tour to see the Old Town.
text: Wonderful  site seeing in the Old Town. Grand Plaza - museums - churches and various shops well worth a day walk about. Although somewhat crowded occasionally felt safe everywhere. A ‘ not miss’ outing in Quito.........with some good coffee to be found in  numerous locations.

title: Great walking tour
text: Nice city centre, walking through history and seeing the mix of cultures all around. Friendly place, great shops.

title: Great city
text: A wonderful , colourful place to visit. Beware that it is very hilly and you need your walking shoes to get th3 best out of this city. A local guide will take you to all the most interesting places.

title: Charming Old Town
text: We enjoyed wandering the streets of the Old Town of Quito, and learning a bit about the history and visiting the beautiful churches and the plazas. Quito is a beautiful city, and if visiting this old town is a "must see".

title: Walkable, safe, fascinating
text: The churches, plazas, restaurants, street vendors, etc etc... so much to see and enjoy.  We spent 5 days in Quito in December  and are so glad we chose a hotel in Old Town. Some reviews suggested safety could be an issue but we never once felt concerned.  Be sensible and enjoy the history.

title: Very interesting and good place to people watch
text: Dropped off by our guide and walked around for several hours. Very interesting places and stores to visit. Lots of security - at no time did we feel threatened or hustled. Also easy access to public bathrooms. Found a shop with wonderful ice cream - although a bit pricey for Ecuador it is 'big city'.   Would gladly visit again

title: The whole area around the plaza is filled with:
text: Sights, sounds, smellsLocals and touristsTrinkets and upscale shopsRestaurants and cafesColors and peeling paint/plasterTraffic of cars and peopleChurches, galleries and museums

title: Lovely Old City
text: The contrast between the old and new Quito is stark, with both having their pros and cons, but the old part is truly a historic area.  Lovely squares and architecture that reflects a bygone age with plenty of places to eat and drink or stay.

title: Excellent for a long stroll
text: The Quito Old Town is a great area for strolling. The area is large and therefore you can stroll for an entire day. There are many churches to explore and fabulous plazas we can get ice cream or have your shoes shined.Don’t miss La Rhonda with its quirky artisan shops & great restaurants.

title: Old Town - Ecadour
text: Find a tour to support your enjoyment of the Old Town in Quito.  Lots to discover. Churches, markets and tastes of Ecuador.  Gives you a good orientation and you will find most hostels on the edge of this area.

title: Must see
text: Ecuador has done an excellent job preserving this old city.  Very walkable and lots to see.   While safe, there are a huge number of locals and tourists around.  Be sure not to flash valuables and keep everything safe.   There was one area close to one of the vey big churches where the prostitutes ply their trade. It's legal.  Look  (or don't look) for the women in tight clothing with the umbrellas

title: Priceless & unmatched
text: Quito is by far the best preserved colonial town in this part of the world. Start from the Plaza Grande and just wander around without a plan. Enter all the streets and narrow passes. In each one you will find something different and interesting. The area is fully secure. Never mind about what you may read in tourist guides or what you may hear from other people. Ecuatorian Police is everywhere.

title: Walking Tour and Hop on Bus
text: We spent an enjoyable four hours walking around the Plaza Grande and surrounding area. Busy, crowded and interesting. Always felt safe but do pay attention to your surroundings and personal security. Friendly people, great food and shopping experience.

title: on the weekend it's great for street art, from music to sculpture
text: I go to enjoy watching people. Today there was a crowd singing a beautiful song together. I think it was a praise song because they were surrounding a guy with a Bible in his hand who also held a microphone. This was in one of the large plazas.

title: A must see in the city
text: Beautiful buildings and full of history. I recommend to find a free walking tour to visit. It is not the safest area, so you need to stay alert!

title: Shoe shine
text: A very pretty and safe enclave surrounded by amazing colonial architecture and incredible churches. We really enjoyed just walking round and stopping for a coffee or beer. In the main square and numerous shoe shining engineers - would recommend!!

title: Many interesting historic buildings and churches within easy walking
text: The old town is quite compact which makes in a practical proposition to explore on foot. There are many historic buildings which can often only be viewed from outside. Some exceptions like the city library and of course, many churches allow entry.Caution though. It is easy to stray a couple of blocks into less touristy areas, where crime levels increase markedly. If there aren't a lot of tourists around, maybe you shouldn't be.

title: Mugged at 3:30 pm on Christmas at Knifepoint
text: Not sure Ecuador is a safe place. My wife and I are celebrating our honeymoon and the first day in Quito we were robbed by two men @ Knife Point. This was in broad daylight on Christmas. Both of us have traveled extensively in NIcaragua, Cambodia, Laos, Thailand, Eastern Europe, Argentina, Chile etc. What I am most profoundly disappointed in is the apathy of the Ecuadorian People. Some guy is swinging a knife at me and another guy is tackling my wife both arms around her and no one does a damn thing. I simply cannot believe that no one said anything. We are scheduled for 2 weeks here but we may be looking for an audible.

title: Walkable and safe
text: Very walkable and safe. Some traffic at rush hour but overall calm and accessible with lots of insta worthy moments

title: Great colonial city
text: Quito’s old town is a great example of a colonial city in the americas. Filled with cobblestone streets, public squares and old churches, you have to go here if you have some time in Quito. One tip: watch out for large buses on those narrow roads. A member of our group almost got clipped by one at one point.

title: You need at least two full days and that not enough
text: Well worth a visit!! Great squares, churches and museums. We stayed three nights in a hotel on the hill overlooking the old town and walked down every day. The little streets are so picturesque with lots of old architecture. Also the people are so friendly and even telling us to be careful when I was using my phone to navigate. But never encountered any problems. Although at night time we always used a taxi which are relatively cheap.

title: So much to see
text: Exploring Quito's old town and all the churches, museums and shops was a highlight. Find the 7 crosses - it's like a really interesting scavenger hunt.

title: Amazing place.
text: This was amazing to see, nestled in the Andes. So many beautiful buildings to see and excellent to see on a walking tour or by yourself.

title: The place to visit in Ecuador!
text: A beautiful city with a fascinating history, beautiful scenery, and where the global equator was discovered and you can stand on it with  memorable experience!

title: Short tour of old city
text: Had a personalized tour as I had limited time .  The company DC Tours recommended by the Wyndham Garden Quito Manager.  The owner of the tour company Daniel Cando was excellent.  A must if you are in the city. Visited Panecillo Hill.  Beautiful view.  Architecture and churches are beautiful.  Driver spoke English fluently and knows the city well . Paid upfront and then he took me me to the airport hotel after the tour.

title: Quito, pasión por la cultura
text: Beautyfull gardens, plazas and old restored houses make Old Town Quito very special. Visit Mote Colonial. Have great views to Panecillo Hill

title: Nice walk
text: Lots of different things to do and see. Not great for shopping, but who cares about it when there is so much, right?

title: Lots of history
text: Nice & close to lots of historical buildings, nice neighborhoods too. Chance to people watch & see a lot of the locals

title: A place to go
text: I went to Quito for business. I think Quito is the most overlooked destination that I have been in my life. I have been to Rome and Paris, New York, Nice, and many other places and I was not expecting Quito to be so beautiful and the culture to be so rich. I didn’t have to much time to see the sites but the time I did have was amazing. The people are amazing and the city was safe. We walked most places even at night and had nothing but kind helpful people everywhere. It is an amazing place and I would recommend anyone and all to make this great place a destination. One warning* the city sits at (approximately) 10,000 feet altitude. It didn’t bother me but it may bother some.

title: Good Old Town
text: This Old Town was crowded when I was there, but I did enjoy the atmosphere of the week-end among the tourists and local people. The buildings were very antiques and had long history that you can learn. Unfortunately I have been there for a very short time before went to the airport. I will be back if there is any opportunity in the future.

title: A World Heritage City
text: Quito will not disappoint you.The city of Quito is build on many mountains and within ravines, situated in the Andes of Ecuador. The climate is ideal, although this time of year, are daily down pours of rain in the afternoon.

title: Old Town wanderings
text: Very crowded and can be dangerous to cross streets. Much local color and traditional food and markets.  Taxis everywhere.

title: Old style, which is nice a quite, with plenty of charm
text: You can get some nice food and great pictures, without being hassled. plenty to see and do, I recommend hitting up one of the local places for breakfast, around $2 for everything

title: A lot of security.
text: Weekends are the best to visit.  They block off the surrounding streets on Sunday and there are a lot of booths.

title: A good tourist destination
text: Well worth a visit!! Lots to see in this area.... cathedral churches museums la ronda's quirky little streets! Watch out for the occasional street theatre performance! If you wish to visit the Presidential Palace you need to prebook you will need your passport!

title: Things we have to do in Quito old town (casco viejo )
text: Walk at night, it´s safe. Eat food street ( chaulafan, salchipapas ) it´s tasty. Go to the central market to taste ecuatorian breakfast. Take buses and trolebuses among local people. Eat and dance at La Ronda street. Go to the Panecillo. Seat in a bench in any square and pay atention to people´s daily routine.

title: Beautiful area well worth the visit
text: Don’t miss this lovely historic part of Quito. It’s a beautiful part of the city with many magnificent buildings and churches to visit. You can also grab a delicious meal in the famous grand plaza hotel restaurant where downstairs they serve a superb Equadorian meal. The waiter service there is also outstanding. The square in the centre is always busy with people and full of atmosphere. Really the nicest part of the city.

title: Must Do
text: Of course while here you must do a city tour, we found it fun and informative, we visited local markets and restaurants to small coffee shops. Enjoyed the culture.

title: beauty in Quito
text: Walking around the area one can get a sense of the history in the buildings. The designs are amazing and are standing the test of time. A must see place when in town.

title: Worth the time
text: Given the mess created by the combination of small streets and too many vehicles, it may take awhile to finally get to the old town of Quito.   But its definitely worth it.  The architecture is a great example of colonial New World thinking.  High ceilings, ornate facades and beautiful inner courtyards reflect the old Spanish influences.  Add in the amazing number of functioning churches and it becomes obvious that the Spanish influence has evolved into its own Ecuadorian style.  Strongly recommended = the San Francisco Church during a Mass, Compania de Jesus (Jesuit) church for its gold lattice work in the ceiling and walls, and the stained glass windows in the National Basilica.Keep in mind- the Metropolitan Cathedral is actually closed on Sunday (imagine that...)

title: Wonderful street life!
text: Old Quito is filled with amazing colonial buildings, great museums and churches, and colourful, lively street life!

title: You must visit the old town when you're in Quito
text: In the old town, you will see the central square, which is stately, and there is a lot of beautiful colonial-era architecture throughout the area.  Be prepared for a lot of walking, much uphill.  It's worth your time to just wander around and look at the buildings and churches.

title: Architectural buffet
