text: Walking through the Old Town was a feast for the eyes for architectural enthusiasts.  One can't help but admire how these old buildings were preserved.  Even the hotel Cantuna we stayed at in the heart of Historical town was an old Nunnery, intact with a chapel, original wooden and tile  floors and columns and antique decorations, door knobs and handles wall fixtures and furnitures/armoires

title: you could easily spend days wandering here!
text: This is the best area of the city. so many nooks and crannies to explore, and many meals to enjoy! The people watching can't be beat!

title: Good place to walk and explore
text: The old city has many beautiful churches and other historic buildings. I saw police officers everywhere and felt safe when walking and exploring.  On Sunday they closed one of the squares to auto traffic and families came by riding their bicycles.  Taxis are easily available.  I speak Spanish so I had no difficulty.

title: Very few tourists, just lots of local folk and friendly atmosphere
text: We totally enjoyed wandering around Quito’s historic centre listening to street music, people watching, and discovering great places to eat (see my review of Plaza Cafe Grande). There are plenty of churches to step into to fine quiet or just rest.

title: Walking tour through old town Quito
text: The tourist information provides a map with several short walking routes; they lead along all the major sites - we spent a day walking all the routes.

title: busy old town with loads of souvenirs, local snacks and specialty stores
text: our guide walked us through this busy part of town. we were presented with many souvenirs by street hawkers and were impressed by the leather goods and art and craft goods. must visit.

title: Love love love
text: Simply one of the most beautiful Centro Histórico I’ve ever been! The plaza grande is amazing full of locals it’s a good way to start people watching!

title: La Ronda is a great place to visit!
text: We've been to La Ronda twice, and we'll be going there again. There's something about the vibe that continues to draw us in.

title: Quaint, historical and spiritual.
text: Quito's old town is a pleasure to visit. There is the town square, the presidential palace, the Basillica and churches. Simply charming!

title: So much to see!!
text: A walking tour of Old Town Quito with a guide is the only way to go; otherwise, you will miss all the nooks and crannies where interesting things happened during the long colonial history of the city!

title: Walking is a pleasure
text: The Historical District is wonderful to visit. Much to see. Avenue of Seven Churches. Many museums and tons of people all over. The energy is thrilling and fun to watch.

title: Old Town
text: So much to see and do! Great for walking and people watching. Took a walking tour that helped get me oriented.  Wonderful restaurants too.
The best old town in south america. I really had a good time everything is cheap. Use sunblock, and a pair of shades,

title: A beautiful town
text: If you visit Quito, be sure to explore the Old Town.  Alpaca shawls are beautiful and quite a bargain here.

title: Short visit to know Quito
text: Very interesting with all those old houses and so many museums and different activities like the Change of Guard.

title: interesting
text: had a free tour with a guide from friends hostel. interesting. went up the towers of the basilica - high....

title: Historic area
text: Old Town has some special architecture to view as you stroll as well as a variety of places to stop in and visit - including the Presidential Palace.

title: nice walk for shopping; eating tradional ecuadorian food and learn history.
text: nice walk; to observe Ecuadorian culture and talk to real people. Nice souvenirs and amazing food. Very colonial full of history.

title: A true heritage centre
text: A must to see some of the well preserved and restored heritage churches and buildings 9f central Quito. It has all the tourist trappings but still features old style street markets and merchants with some local food experiences. A vibrant and festive area for all generations.

title: Great Old City
text: City buildings makes it seem like your walking thru a museum. Lots of old buildings and sights.Take a day and walk about. Do the main sights and still lots more to explore.Best do a half day tour with a guide. Then take the evening and explore more outside of the main attractions.

title: Unexpected gem!
text: The city of flowers and UNESCO world heritage as well as our host Any (Casa de la Gringa in La Floresta) made our time in Quito very special! The old town was definitely one of the highlights of our stay.After browsing through rather discouraging online travel reviews, we did not expect the beauty and good vibe of this unique city. We would have loved to stay longer and will definitely return to Quito and Ecuador soon! Again, we learned a lot about the past and present of the place on a great free walking tour in the old town including interesting insights on why Ecuador does not have it’s own currency (originally Sucre, nowadays USD) for almost 20 years and that they once managed to have 3 presidents in 5 month. Also, their president lives in the city centre next to the mayor, the most expensive and cheapest hotel are right next to each other and they built a new subway from the north to the south part of Quito (40km) in 6 years which will be officially opened in 2020 (check this out Canada and Germany;))!!!Despite of many political disputes and challenges Quito city centre has changed a lot especially in the last two years. If you have the chance go and enjoy the place first hand! From our perspective it beats the among tourists and backpackers much more promoted Medellín easily! We used all kinds of public transport and walked a lot and always felt save and comfortable. The people are incredible kind and supportive. Nevertheless, use common sense and be aware of your surroundings especially after sunset as you would in every other mayor city in Central and South America!

title: Wonderful to stroll around
text: The photos speak for themselves, a very friendly city with lots of beautiful old colonial architecture.

title: Fun to Walk Around
text: We stayed about a 10 minute walk from the Old Town, and walked in the Old Town a lot, and enjoyed it.  We went on a Free Tour (Strawberry Tours), visited some museums (there are a ton), a couple churches, the Basilica, a couple squares, etc.  We ate a couple times in a restaurant on the 3rd floor of a building on the Plaza Grande.  There are much cheaper places to eat on the first floor.  I even got my hair cut at a place on the ground floor where there are some shops at the palace on the main square.  She did a great job for $8 (plus a tip).  The squares are good for people watching too.

title: Toured Quito Old Town via the bus tour
text: I've taken this bus tour twice. It is a MUST DO item to check off your list while visiting Quito. Lots to learn and see. The price is amazing!!

title: Great tour sponsored by Celebrity Cruises
text: Celebrity took our group to the old town and provided an incredible lunch upstairs in a fabulous restaurant overlooking the square downtown.  From the square you can look up the hill and see the "virgin" statue in the distance, and a beautiful view of the city.  It is a colonial looking old city.  Old churches; which of course the most expensive looking buildings are the 2.  We had a guard with the group, as apparently there are pickpockets in the area.  We never felt unsafe, or aware of them if they were there.  As with any city; beware.  Very high altitude in Quito; 9000 ft. or more, but we didn't have much problem with that.  Definitely loved QUito.  Weather was perfect.

title: Churches and shopping, lots of people.
text: Load of Churches literally on every corner Colorful people selling stuff cheap. Beware of the guy selling maps though. Bring your own T.P and put it in the trash, not potty.

title: Very clean and beautiful town
text: Beautiful at night, I visited at 9:30pm on a week day and the streets were almost empty. Go for a nice walk, I felt secure...

title: Incredibly beautiful architecture
text: Quito has developed on some amazing geological forms. Steep hills and deep ravines are plainly visible when one arrives by air, and on the ground the old town contains many steep narrow streets, a multitude of ornate churches, museums, small shops and business, street sellers, musicians, bars and cafes and inevitably a conflagration of traffic. Surprisingly though the air quality did not seem so polluted and our walks through the narrow streets did not result in any throat or chest problems. However, the altitude can have it's own effect and for the first day headaches may occur due to the reduction of oxygen. In spite of an undesirable reputation for street crime, Quito is patrolled by many tourist and national police which gives one a certain confidence but visitors should still be reminded to take precautions with their valuables and use some protection against the very strong overhead sunlight.

title: Beatiful old city center
text: The old town of Quito is realy very beautiful. It has been renovated with care. Therfore you really have the impression how the colonial fouders have buil;d the town. More and more the center is car free and this makes a stroll around the town very pleasant. Ther are many beuatiful museums and churches. Especially the great squari in the center is nice to visit or to take a coffe

title: Beautiful Old Town
text: My family and I love walking through the streets of Quitos Old town, the architecture is beautiful to look at. The locals are so friendly and make the trip even more special.
Take a good guide and explore the history of Quito. The plaza, the cathedrals, people, food is quite extraordinary.

title: Worth a full day, plus some
text: We spent parts of three days touring the Old Town.  Plenty to see and experience.  Several ver the top churches.  Should have spent some of the gold on the people.  Be prepared for hills and lots of people.  Never felt unsafe or observed obvious pick pockets.  Very heavy security.  Didn’t see any restaurants that called to us.

title: Spare time
text: It was a great surprise about my business trip!I enjoy a lot this visit where i could find beautiful architecture, history and great people.Quito give you a wonderful opportunity to see the real history from the European colonization in South America

title: Beautiful architecture
text: The old town is definitely worth a visit with its old churches, buildings and plazas. Be prepared for some steep streets but worth the walk.

title: A Swarming Hive of Folks
text: Vendors, hawkers, merchants, entertainers, guides, families, tourists make Quito's Old City a bustling hive of activity and noises, especially on a Saturday. Cathederals everywhere, shoes shops galore, fabric stores, hostels, clothing, food vendors, ice cream hawkers, you name it. You will definitely be neither starve nor bored for an instant in the Old City. I've been four times in 2 weeks, both by cab and by bus. I recommend the later for ease and speed, but be prepared for a Mr.Toad's wild ride. This is an accessible, well used city. I think you could buy anything there from hardware to religious icons, to Panama hats that are really Ecuadorean. Some museums are free. Expects crowds and a fascinating excursion, and uphill climbs that level out before you drop.

title: Interesting and fine by day
text: There are guided tours of Old Town but we opted to just wander around. This is fine for day time but not recommended at night. On Sundays there are food carts, street performers and lots of families visiting the parks. Use of a guidebook or map to pre- plan is certainly helpful. Waving a map around when your walking is an invitation for tour guides and worse. There are several old churches with very different architectural styles, as well as just the buildings and parks themselves. We spent a nice afternoon and you should certainly try the helado (ice cream).

title: Amazing historic district, lots of beautiful colonial architecture
text: Very walkable, wonderful place to visit. Keep your pockets zipped as you enjoy this expansive neighborhood. Lots of churches, squares, and alleys to explore. Plenty of restaurants and coffee shops, and shopping.

title: Must do!
text: Our guide took us here in the morning and some places were very uncrowned.  We walked through the streets and had a lovely experience.   A must do!

title: Pittoresque
text: Very cute, definitely worth visiting!A must if  you are visiting Quito!Many churches, museums and coffee shops.

title: Fabulous
text: This old town has so much to offer and the history is amazing with 2 gold churches which you can’t miss. We loved being there on a Sunday as it’s closed to cars, so easy to get around.

title: Historic and beautiful
text: It’s like stepping back in time. Beautiful buildings, great food! Take a guided tour to get the full effect of the experience.

title: Hit the Highlights
text: We had a map picked up at hotel (or airport.)  It lists highlights.  If you have a relatively short time, see them.  It seems that will give the flavor of the Historic Old Town.  A tour guide can be helpful for more info.  Don't miss going into La Compania.

title: Beautiful!
text: The old town is beautiful - lots of stunning building with a real Spanish / Catalan feel. Several stunning churches as well. Watch out for the pickpockets though!

title: good for a day out or passing Quito
text: Visit the plaza of independence but be careful for the religious crazies around they can be quite abrupt, but the local metro police are around to put them in check great cafes & also a tourist point on calle venezuela , you also have montufar and other shops around but again be careful with thieves.

title: Highly recommend
text: Tidied up over the last 20 years. Seems safer ( from Pickpockets).Lots of cafes, access to churches. Even guides available.Lovely buildings and good atmosphere.

title: Very Safe near the Plaza Grande and Calle Ronda
text: There are police officers everywhere near the plaza Grande and Calle Ronda. We visited the church, Compania ( The interior has 120 lbs of Gold ), and walked on Calle Ronda. Shopped for chocolate and soaps made with exotic fruits. We had dinner at Casa Geranium, during the evening

title: You don’t need a tour
text: I walked all around the museums and churches markets, much better than be in a group , taxis are really cheap also. Take your time here as there are really great shops also. The people are friendly and helpful with foreign you don’t need to be shy.

title: An easy day’s touring...
text: We loved Quito Old Town - full of life and lots of history. We were lucky enough to visit during the Festival de la Luz (National Independence celebrations) but have been back since and it’s equally fun!I’d highly recommend getting a tour guide as it’s hard to spot the right places to go - particularly if Spanish isn’t your first language - but if you’re determined to go it alone don’t miss the Big Square, and make sure you try some local Desayunos (breakfast) and Almuerzos (Lunch of the Day) in one of the many cafes. If you do fancy a tour guide, there’s a brilliant free tour from the Friends Hostel if you’re staying there.Two quick warnings: the old town is famous for its pickpockets so lock up your bag and wear it on your front (even the locals do). It’s perfectly safe and we never felt hassled, but it’s not worth taking the risk. Also, look around for a good deal on food - the places directly on the tourist hot spots tend to be much more expensive than even a few streets away.Top Tip: go on a Monday at 11am if you can, which means you can watch the changing of the Presidential Guard.

title: Fascinating History
text: We walked through Old Town with our group and a local guide who told us stories about the history of the city.  We also toured Plaza Francisco to learn about the liberation of the country from Spain.  We particularly enjoyed a tour of the Baroque Church, which was heavily damaged by fire a few years ago.  They have to charge a small admission fee now to help pay for the restoration. Every surface of the church is covered with beautiful gold leaf...it’s very impressive.  We liked the two large paintings hanging in the entry area where non church members used to have to stand.  One depicts hell and the sins that can send someone there.  The other depicts the judgement day.  Very powerful images.

title: Quito Old Town
text: Great, colorful area.  Sample the history and architecture of Quito.  Friendly people and great food.  Lovely gardens.

title: Nice to walk around
text: It is impressive!At nights with the ilumination effect, it is more beautifull tho see!It is a must a walk at nights! Safe until around 10pm!

title: Old Town, Calle La Runda and Panecillo
text: Quito is a really nice Town. Their a lot of Churches, Cathedrales, Plazas and Markets. Calle La Runda is really beautiful and with a Taxi or a Busride (only 25 Cent) you will quickly reach the Statue of Virgin Maria on the top of Panecillo

title: Ideal Neighborhood
text: Ideal neighborhood to walk around and enjoy Quito. The buildings are all interesting to look at with the diversity of architecture. Clean with a good amount of restaurants and places to grab a drink. I would caution being around here during the late night. Had two run ins with groups of people who were trying to cause trouble. Granted, it was midnight... so just heads-up!

title: step back into 13 century with Grittiness 21st century
text: Time has served this old city well , the community appears to value this space and its returning value , weekends and holidays families flock to the area to simply watch humanity walk by , while street entertainers , vendors pedal their wares . Local youths can pose a problem for small pick pockets and  horse play

title: Do a walk to appreciate it's history and architecture
text: Take a guided tour or a self guided walk. Well worth it, to appreciate the charm of the old town.Gets busy during the day but in the evenings it's almost deserted. Reminded us of Havana. Lots of tourist police at all the main areas so you feel safe but still be sensible. Lots of churches, eateries and shops but virtually no bars, if that's your thing you'll struggle to find somewhere to sit and drink alcohol.

title: Gorgeous and Cosmopolitan
text: I really enjoyed touring the old town, and it felt so European to me. I really enjoyed people watching, shopping, and viewing all the Spanish colonial architecture.

title: Nice place to look around
text: With cobbled streets and painted buildings this is a lovely part of town to wander around. Lots of shops selling goods, shoes, gifts and cloth to make clothing or table clothes.Is pretty square to sit in with blossoming trees is a perfect place to stop with an ice cream from one of the shops and enjoy the sights and sounds of this lovely town.

title: Very quiteño
text: Very nice to walk around, clean, mostly safe and preserved. It’s recommendable to try some typical ecuatorian dishes.

title: Quito old town
text: The old city in Quito is an awesome place to visit. Highly suggest staying at carpe diem and also using thier travel agent Capedm Adventures

title: Good Food, Great people and Amazing Culture!
text: Worth a walk through the old town. You will not see any tourist shops or find any souvenirs here; it is all authentic Ecuadorian Culture.The smell of good food and colourful shops fill the streets. Worth a visit!

title: Charming
text: Very spiritual are with cobble stoned hilly streets.  We listened to a band play near the San Francisco plaza.  Street vendors with a variety of original artwork.

title: Colonial Old Town
text: Well preserved colonial part of Quito. Definitely worth a day of exploring for the beautiful architecture. There are a couple of museums that are a nice, short diversion. Lots of places to eat and shop make this a great day out.

title: Blown away by the beauty.
text: 100% stunned by the extensive beauty of old town Quito. It's a large area and comparable to any of the best historic districts of Europe.  The view of the mountains surrounding town are amazing and the architecture is constantly surprising and stunning. Make sure to find the streets that have no or little traffic.  This city is a gem.

title: Must do, but ...
text: It is small and I found not super interesting. I never thought I would say this, but it could be a little more tourist friendly.Lots of shops for locals.For a limited time Yoko Ono has an exhibit here and its free. I found it interesting.There are some good places for lunch and probably dinner too, but I didn’t stay that long.

title: If possible go on a monday
text: Mondays are the best for visiting the Old Town as you can watch the changing of the guard at the presidential palace.  It can get crowded so watch your pocket books.  Many shops off the main square and lots of people selling items ripe for tourists.

title: Stunning
text: Beautiful and well preserved old town, it's a complete must when visiting Ecuador. Never felt unsafe.

title: The Biggest and better preserved colonial town in America
text: You will feel like you just got back in time walking on the narrow streets of Old town. You will find a lot of culture, mixture between spanish and Inca culture, awesome churches and lots and lots of art.

title: The historical section of town
text: Old Town dates back to the Incas, and after them the Spanish colonial period. It is a nice area with multiple churches, historical buildings, restaurants, and shopping. We spent the whole day exploring the area without any difficulties. Just remember not to dress flashy or wear expensive and obvious jewelry, use common sense, and you'll be fine. There are many stores for Ecuadorian items. They also make great leather shoes, which are sold at bargain prices. The area has many restaurants that serve meals for anywhere from $3 and up. We stopped at one and had half Pollo Asado, papas, and a drink for $3.50. Its a nice area to get the feel of Quito. As always in Quito places that take credit are limited. Cash is king, only take what you think you will need for the day.

title: Every year gets more friendly and safer.
text: Things has gotten progressively better over the last 6 years with much less crime, a happy atmosphere, and families walking the street, enjoying the festivities with smiles on their faces. The central square is comfortable with lots of seating and people watching, several churches to see, and neat shops to look at. Loved walking the streets but there are steep hills, but worth the climbs. We stayed downtown for the first time and it was worth it.

title: Quito histórico
text: Very nice area with many attractions like the different churches and the museum! They have also a variety of shops where you can buy different products made from wool or similar items made in Ecuador.  Also went to the Mercado Mariscal and saw many shops and both a sweater and a hat made in Ecuador.  You can get chocolate products or coffees. Really enjoyed my stay visited many churches like the Iglesia de Santo Domingo, San Francisco convent and others. Recommend you visit the area in your trip.

title: Old Town Quito - first World Heritage Site
text: Our guide Jose of HappyGringo took us on a long half-day tour of Quito's Old Town on our second day in Quito.  It was an informative tour of a beautiful Old Town that dates back to the 1500's, and that only includes the Spanish part, built on the ruins of the Inca city that superseded the 1000 year old city before it, the one that the Incas themselves destroyed in advance of the Spanish conquest.   The Old Town has survived earthquakes, volcanoes, war, and political change.   It is extremely photogenic, with well-maintained buildings still in use, set against the dramatic background of both the cordillera of volcanic Andean Peaks and the basin of the supervolcano of which the individual volcanoes are but discrete eruptions.  Since the Old Town is approximately the same elevation as most of Quito, around 9000 to 9500 feet above sea level, a tour of it is a good choice for a flatlander like myself to spend shortly after arriving in Quito.

title: Quito old town was one of our excursions.
text: We took a double floor dutch bus and enjoyed all fothe sights in Quito.. including Quito's old town and saw plenty of historical buildings and churches.

title: Quaint
text: Old Quito is quite a sight. Old churches, cobbled streets, myriad changes in level. An amazing place to explore. Highly recommended

title: Great spot for families
text: Visited with kid. It was great! He loved chasing pigeons and taking in the sights. It was busy and mid-afternoon with lots of shade. Was a little on the cool side.

title: Interesting
text: If you visit Quito, you can't skip this. A nice walk through some historical sites. Very interesting architecture.

title: Architecture is cool — remember it rains in July
text: Kids were good with a walk through historical Quito. They loved the lunch place - and we saw a lot of cool architecture. We came mid morning (teens like to sleep) and weather app proved correctly -it poured rain. We found little shops to duck into but do watch app. It made for a soggy and shorter trip.

title: Nice spot
text: Nice place to stroll. Many churches and in a former monastry we tasted handmade ice. Really relaxing place.

title: Just do it
text: It was superb to see the old town and the spanish influence that built it. I thoroughly enjoyed the architecture. I wish they had some more interactive displays to give a better idea of the history you were viewing.
